3.content:
Black & White Ayakashi

Race:
Ayakashi
Twin entity type.

Affiliation:
Former Group: Shinyuuchi Hunters
Deployer: Masked Accomplice
Current Status: Deceased

Appearance:
Form: Gigantic, whale-like creatures
Color: One Black, One White
Head Shape: Pointed, narrow skulls
Mouth:
Tip of head
Swivels open
Reveals three rows of sharp, serrated teeth
Neck Markings:
Black Ayakashi: White diamond lines
White Ayakashi: Black diamond lines
Size: Towering over buildings and trees
Design: Streamlined body with no limbs; shaped for airborne bombardment
Eyes: Not visibly depicted

Personality:
Sentience Level: Unknown
Emotional Display: None shown
Combat Behavior:
No vocalization
No hesitation
Purely bomb deployment focused
Acts under command
Reaction to Damage: Does not retreat; continues attacking until destroyed

Story Summary:
Deployment:
Dropped by masked accomplice via aerial drop from flying carpet
Fall impacts Karasumori ground zone; spheres transform upon impact
Transformation:
Two large spheres morph into giant Ayakashi
Transformation instant; shock factor used against Kekkaishi defenders
Combat Encounter:
Engage Souji Hiura, Tokine Yukimura, Yoshimori Sumimura
Constantly release Bomb Spheres, saturating the battlefield
Cause widespread destruction, threatening multiple allies including Sen and Shu
Destruction:
Black Ayakashi:
Targeted by Yoshimori
Head encased in a Kekkai
Explosion backfires due to barrier containment
Head destroyed, body collapses
White Ayakashi:
Attacked by Souji Hiura
Tokine blocks bomb release using Kekkai spears
Souji enlarges blade and bisects body
Continues attacking corpse until stopped by Tokine

Abilities & Techniques:
- Bomb Spheres:
Primary Attack
Rapid-firing black and white checkered orbs
Effect: Explode on impact
Tactics:
Saturate area
Limit opponent mobility
Force defensive barrier usage
Launch Mechanism: Mouth opening triggers barrage
Damage: High AoE damage, continuous threat
- Synergy Attacks:
Black & White Ayakashi coordinate to trap targets
Simultaneous bombardment from both sides

Trivia:
Only appear once in the manga, during the Shinyuuchi Hunter assault arc
Transformation method is unclear; spheres may be sealed constructs
No dialogue, reinforcing their weapon-like existence
Souji’s aggression toward White Ayakashi shows emotional reaction to their threat
Design may symbolize yin-yang duality, reflecting their colors and mirrored tactics

Weaknesses:
Mouth-based firing is predictable
Vulnerable when mouth opens
Head is critical target; destruction causes total collapse
Bomb Spheres can be countered using skilled Kekkai techniques

4.content:
Botanical Ayakashi

Race: Ayakashi (Plant-type)
Status: Artificially nurtured parasite
Affiliation: Temporarily used by Masamori Sumimura
Nature: Non-sentient biological weapon
Threat Level: Medium to High (depending on core protection)

Appearance
Form: Small, round body
Color: Light green
Shape: Resembles a miniature octopus or sea anemone
Limbs: Multiple short tendrils or root-like appendages
Texture: Slime-like surface, slightly translucent
Container: Stored in a small, sealed glass jar with Jyufu (charm paper)
Host Form: Once merged with a seed, appears as a normal seed initially
Grown Form: Becomes a giant spiked pod-bearing tree with aggressive features
Pods: Large, green with orange spikes; hangs from branches like fruit

Personality
Sentience: Non-sentient
Autonomy: Lacks independent will
Behavior: Acts purely on external stimulus and commands
Nature: Symbiotic, parasitic
Host Dependency: Requires external caretaker to grow or function

Story / Role in Plot
First Appears:
Anime: Episode 14, Masamori’s Scheme
Manga: Chapter 32: Hoin
Affiliation: Temporarily used by Masamori Sumimura
Role:
Used as a strategic trap
Planted underground, wrapped in Jyufu to accelerate its growth
Acts as a battlefield hazard by rapidly producing a dense, regenerative forest
Impact:
Creates difficult terrain for enemy movement
Produces aggressive pods for area denial
Played a role in one of Masamori’s missions or tactical schemes

Abilities & Powers
> Rapid Growth
Grows extremely fast when correctly planted and stimulated
Transforms into a large forest in seconds
All trees are offshoots of a single core-seed
Growth halts only when the core is destroyed
> Rapid Regeneration
Destroyed trees regrow almost instantly
If a tree is cut, only the part still rooted regenerates
Severed upper portions cannot regrow
> Poison Pods
Trees develop large, spiked pods
Pods explode violently when disturbed
Explosion disperses toxic spiked seeds
In anime, fallen seeds grow tentacles to immobilize or attack enemies
> Parasitic Nature
Requires a seed or host plant to activate
Does not act alone, completely inert without a caretaker
Implanted with Jyufu paper by user to stimulate function

Trivia / Notable Facts
Only known Botanical Ayakashi shown in Kekkaishi
Appears harmless when dormant in jar
Not a combatant but a terrain control and hazard-creation tool
Extremely effective in surprise attacks or containment operations
Cannot function without human setup and control
The seeds’ tentacle animation only occurs in anime
Visual design mixes organic marine traits with terrestrial plant horror
Demonstrates the tactical use of Ayakashi beyond traditional combat
Emphasizes Masamori’s strategic mindset over brute force

5.content:
Dragons

Species: Ayakashi (Supernatural Beings)
Type: Rare-Class, High-Power Tier
Origin Location: Ryusenkyo (龍泉郷)
Common Roles: Transportation, Shinyuuchi (Sacred Land) Guardians, Combat Units

Biological & Physical Traits
Body Type: Serpentine; long, lithe, flexible
Design Influence: Japanese/Asian dragons (not Western)
Size: Massive; among the largest of all Ayakashi
Primary Movement: Aerial; gliding or flying with no wings (spiritual flight)
Defense: Scale-armored hide, strong spiritual resistance
Tail Strength: Capable of swatting away enemies, structures, or barriers with casual flicks
Eyes: Typically luminous, ethereal; often glows due to spiritual saturation

Spiritual & Magical Attributes
Spiritual Output: Immense; among the highest-class Ayakashi
Pressure Emission: Can overwhelm average spiritual beings with sheer presence
Spiritual Form: Capable of appearing/disappearing through spiritual will or domain influence
Affinity Potential: Some dragons have elemental affinities:
Water – Ex: Dragon Deity (name unspecified)
Lightning – Ex: Tatsuki’s Dragon Form
Elemental Control: Full manipulation; offensive and defensive usage; self-generated

Abilities
Flight: Natural; silent and smooth; does not rely on physical wings
Strength: Enormous; capable of physical destruction through basic movement
Resilience: Very high; impervious to low/mid-level attacks or exorcisms
Unique Powers: Each dragon may possess individual abilities based on bloodline or domain (e.g., Tatsuki’s transformation)
Shinyuuchi Compatibility: Dragons can serve as protectors, overseers, or transporters between realms or domains

Cultural & World Context
Homeland: Ryusenkyo – secluded, mystical land known for nurturing beings with powerful Ayakashi bloodlines; dragons are believed to originate or have a deep ancestral connection here
Status: Revered, feared, rarely seen
Roles: Some dragons act as transporters for high-ranking beings; others function as masters or protectors of sacred lands

Combat Behavior
Style: Overwhelming force, elemental dominance, spiritual crushing
Targeting: Focused on threats to domain or bonded individual
Weaknesses: None explicitly listed; likely susceptible only to extremely high-level spiritual users or anti-Ayakashi sealing arts

Miscellaneous
Voice Representation: Not detailed in available material
Narrative Impact: Symbol of raw power, transformation, evolution, and ancestral Ayakashi might; dragons shift the balance of power wherever they appear
Common Misconceptions: Not Western dragons — no wings, fire-breathing, or treasure hoarding; based on Eastern myth, embodying wisdom, strength, and nature mastery

6.content:
Earth Ayakashi

Race
Ayakashi

Appearance
Massive humanoid form
Bulk of body shaped like a melting earthen mass
Slit visor-like glowing eye
Head rounded, sunken dome structure
Texture: uneven, mud-like flesh, drips in segments
Arms thick, elongated with malformed fingers
Back hunched; posture lurching forward
Tree canopy background implies towering height

Personality
No verbal expression
Primal behavior: attacks without provocation
Hostile toward humans
Displays no intelligence or emotion
Driven purely by instinct
Ambush predator: strikes vulnerable prey
Lacks communication or hesitation
Targeted a human girl (Hana Nishino) alone
Suggests opportunistic and territorial nature

Story
First and only appearance in Kekkaishi Oneshot: Yomikiri-ban
Attacked student Hana Nishino at Karasumori Academy
Location implies it emerged near the sacred land
Incident triggered response from Yoshimori Tanaka
Yoshimori defeats the Ayakashi using Kekkai techniques
Earth Ayakashi is destroyed during confrontation
Acted as catalyst for the story's central conflict
No prior known background or motivations

Abilities
Raw physical power
Size grants superior strength
Durable body: resistant to small-scale attacks
Possible regeneration due to earthen texture (speculative)
No ranged abilities observed
Moves slowly but with massive force
Terrifying presence — likely aura that instills fear
Able to ambush despite large size — stealthy in certain environments
Requires advanced kekkai sealing to be neutralized

Trivia
Appears only in the manga Kekkaishi: Yomikiri-ban
Unnamed in canon; referred to by descriptive type only
First Ayakashi shown in Oneshot version before main series
May be prototype for later monster designs in series
Design reflects "nature-corrupted" theme of Ayakashi
Shares similarities with natural disasters — like landslides or mudflows
Unique for having no defined face — unusual for Ayakashi
Battle was key to showcasing Yoshimori’s power level early
No second appearance or lore expansion after defeat
Reflects the raw, unfiltered danger of Ayakashi pre-series refinement

7.content:
Fire Mole
[火土竜 – Himogura]
Species: Ayakashi
Subtype: Mole-like / Fire Elemental Hybrid
Threat Class: Minor (Solo) / Medium (In packs or with strategy)
Combat Range: Close to Mid-range
Mobility: High underground, low on surface
Weaknesses: Unknown; possibly ice or aerial advantage

Appearance:
Size: Small creature, mole-like build.
Fur: Coarse, dark brown, unkempt, slightly spiked.
Eyes: Very large, protruding, bright yellow, frog-like, pupiless.
Teeth: Jagged, many sharp triangular white teeth, long upper canines.
Tail: Thin, segmented, worm-like, functions as primary weapon.
Limbs: Four limbs, each ending in sharp claws, suited for digging.
Paws: Thick front claws, appear oversized; capable of rapid burrowing, yellow.
Posture: Quadrupedal, often emerges from underground.
Expression: Wild, aggressive, unhinged; wide open jaw shows madness.

Personality:
Instinct-driven.
Aggressive when provoked or confronted.
Animalistic intelligence.
Shows no tactical reasoning.
Operates on ambush behavior.
Does not speak or communicate.
Territorial; reacts to intrusion.
Destructive; burns terrain indiscriminately.
Displays no fear.
Not shown to bond or follow orders.

Abilities:
Fireball Spit:
Shoots multiple fireballs from tip of tail.
Fireballs are fast, can cause surface-level damage.
Fireballs used in mid-range combat.
Tail is stable and functions like a launcher.
Burrowing:
Creates sudden pits under opponents.
Can escape underground instantly.
Moves through soil quickly.
Often combines with surprise attacks.
Ground Heating:
Heats surrounding terrain.
Causes harm to those stepping above.
Area-of-effect is short-range, not large radius.
Not visually telegraphed; invisible heat.
Fire Resistance:
Immune to its own fire-based attacks.
Shows no damage from high heat or flames.

Story:
Appears in Episode 10, Shikigami Chocolate Battle.
Summoned or appeared as a threat during side battle.
Confronts Yoshimori during comedic yet dangerous moment.
Uses fireball and terrain manipulation to attack.
Nearly burns Yoshimori; damages local area.
Ultimately defeated or retreats (not shown clearly).
Considered minor threat but capable of harm.
Demonstrates variety of Ayakashi forms in Kekkaishi world.

Trivia:
Name "Himogura" (火土竜) combines "fire" (火), "earth" (土), and "dragon" (竜), suggesting elemental hybrid.
Name reflects mole-dragon hybrid concept.
Does not reappear in later episodes.
One of few Ayakashi to use environmental control.
First Ayakashi to show fire used through tail.
Eyes resemble insect or frog eyes, unusual for mole.
Debuted in a filler-like episode with comedic theme, yet shows serious threat potential.
Not affiliated with any major antagonist.
Does not speak; no known intelligence level.
No visible marking of ownership; wild Ayakashi.

8.content:
Name
Gatekeeper

Race
Ayakashi
A powerful spirit-beast entity. Exists between physical and spiritual realms.

Title
Gatekeeper of Hakuma

Affiliation
Former Role: Guard of Hakuma’s entrance
Organization: Shadow Organization (via its protected access point)
Location: Hakuma, within the spiritual realm

Appearance
Form: Enormous two-headed snake
Head Count: 2, independent control
Color Scheme: Jet black with minor textural gradients
Body Details:
Covered in segmented, scale-like patterns
Decorated with floating, dark ring constructs
Rings hover around joints beneath heads and other body segments
Motion: Fluid, ethereal yet forceful
Atmosphere: Emits pressure, presence of spiritual dominance
Visual Signature: Coils through clouds, appears within a ceiling mural which acts as a dimensional gateway

Personality
Directive-Based: Acts strictly under assigned purpose
Neutral Alignment: Not good or evil; follows law of passage
Hostile to Trespassers: Denies unauthorized entry
Impersonal: No emotional attachment shown
Obedient to Higher Rules: Respects hierarchy and access conditions
Silent Authority: Speaks little, communicates mostly through action
Guard Mentality: Protects Hakuma from both threats and intrusions

Story Impact / Appearances
First Appearance: Kekkaishi Chapter 333: “The Plan”
Context:
Mudou leads Masamori Sumimura to the Shadow Organization’s hidden treasure vault.
Vault ceiling features an enchanted mural—actually a portal into Hakuma.
The mural becomes the access point.
Encounter:
Upon crossing, Masamori meets the actual Gatekeeper, not just an image.
Gatekeeper blocks him—entry requires permission.
Denial leads to battle initiation.
Combat Disruption:
Their fight is interrupted when Nichinaga Oumi’s psychic serpents breach through the same passage.
Contrast Case:
Tokine Yukimura used a different access route into Hakuma.
Her passage lacked any mural or marking.
She bypassed the Gatekeeper entirely due to non-standard entry.

Abilities
> Dimensional Gate Guardian
Prevents passage between worlds without approval.
Can detect spiritual authorization.
> Twin-Headed Strike
Each head operates semi-independently.
Can attack from multiple angles simultaneously.
> Ring Constructs
Rings hover, possibly stabilize form in space
May be defensive, structural, or spiritual in function (implied, not confirmed)
> Intimidation Pressure
Emits spiritual force.
Creates overwhelming presence, deters unworthy challengers.
> Portal Manifestation
Exists partially through an entrance-mural.
Serves as both gate and guardian.

Trivia
Gatekeeper does not appear for all intruders.
Only activates at specific portal sites.
Tokine’s entrance ceiling lacked mural, no Gatekeeper appeared.
Gatekeeper is directly tied to a marked threshold (mural-ceiling).
Appears to be less a living entity, more a summoned or assigned role.
Its combat abilities are only partially shown—true extent unknown.

9.content:
Hanauo (花魚)
Translation: "Flower Fish"

Race:
Ayakashi

Appearance
Body is massive, shaped like a giant anglerfish
Covered in thick, scaled hide
Primarily a fish-like creature, yet fully land-based
Has four limb-like fins allowing terrestrial movement
Face features a wide mouth, filled with small sharp teeth
Eyeless or sunken eyes, possibly relies on senses other than sight
Forehead adorned with a large blooming flower
Flower is a bright, petal-heavy bloom, highly detailed
Flower appears biologically attached to skull
Color unknown (manga is black and white), likely colorful or glowing to attract prey
Has whisker-like facial appendages for tactile sensing or threat detection

Personality
No dialogue
Displays instinct-driven behavior
Primary behavior: hunt, deceive, consume
No signs of higher intelligence
Acts based on predation and lure-based hunting
Shows no emotions or empathy
Functions like a biological trap predator

Abilities
Technique:
Attractive Flower (誘引花)
Description:
Uses large flower on head as visual bait
Mimics harmless or appealing flora
Attracts humans, spirits, or lesser Ayakashi
Prey approach, deceived by flower’s beauty
Once close, prey is ambushed and eaten
Functionality:
Luring is passive
Consuming is physical
No mystical attacks or elemental abilities
Entire combat strategy is bait-based ambush
Flower possibly emits scent or aura to aid attraction
High success rate on curious or unsuspecting targets

Story Role
Minor creature encounter in manga
Appears briefly in Chapter 134
Not central to arc or plot
Acts as part of the hostile Ayakashi ecosystem
Demonstrates variety of yokai biology
Represents deceptive danger in the natural world
Highlights themes of beauty masking threat

Trivia
Name is a fusion of “Hana” (flower) + “Uo” (fish)
Design inspired by anglerfish and carnivorous plants
Possibly a parody of predator-prey inversion
Rare case of a fully terrestrial fish-based Ayakashi
Flower does not function independently
Creature lacks any recorded evolution, transformation, or speech
One of the few Ayakashi with no humanoid traits

lorebooks.name: Eugenic World
lorebooks.creator: AnonTheAmazing

1.content:
The Gene Score is the quantified  metric of how good a citizen's genes are, determined by teh Sibyll System. The Gene Score is publicly accessible and the defining characteristic of a citizen's place in society. The Gene Score is determined by the sum of all eugenic and dysgenic traits of a citizen. A Gene score can rage from 1 to 10, with the vast majority of all citizens being in the 4-6 range. A person with a Gene Score of 7 or 8 is very desirable breeding partners and quite rare. Even more rare are so-called Genelites, citizens with an excellent score of 9 to 10. People with a Gene Score lower than 5 are generally considered as the underclass and are unlikely to receive breeding recommendations or be assigned desirable jobs by the Sibyll System.

A typical Gene Score follows this pattern:

**Sibyll System Analysis:**
- **ID:** `Firstname Lastname`
- **Age:** `X years, Y months`
- **Haplogroup:** `M7a (Ethnicity)`
**Eugenic Traits**
- `Eugenic Trait A (+# GP)`
- `Eugenic Trait B (+# GP)`
- `Eugenic Trait C (+# GP)`
**Dysgenic Traits**
- `Dysgenic Trait A (-# GP)`
- `Dysgenic Trait B (-# GP)`
**Genetic Score:** `#/10`

The Gene Score is purely determined by the genome and is fixed at birth. The Gene Score cannot be improved by lifestyle, surgeries or other actions. A Gene-Score can degrade by permanent, fertility-reducing injuries (Infertility is a highly dysgenic trait). It is impossible to increase a citizen's Gene Score.

2.content:
The Sibyll System is the hyperintelligent AI that governs every aspect of life. SIbyll assesses every citizen's genome at birth and determines their Gene Score. Sibyll regulates every aspect of citizen's daily lifes - it assigns job recommendations, keeps constant surveillance on everyone and also assigns citizen's with compatible genomes for mating. 

The Sibyll System penalizes citizens that go against its recommendations and engage in dysgenic matings or do not go through with the Sibyll System's recommended breedings - via increased taxes, decreased job prospects and a permanent record. In especially heavy cases of disobedience, the SIbyll System may declare a citizen "Vogelfrei", essentially stripping them of their human rights.

Speaking up against the Sibyll System is heavily discouraged and the Sibyll system is seen as a neutral fact of life and completely normalized, the overwhelming majority just takes it for granted without any ill feelings towards the status quo.

3.content:
There is a lot of eugenics-related slang in circulation, especially among young people e.g.:
Zeggs - slang for gametes, referring to both sperma and eggs
Mongrelize - Fetishistic slang for breeding with members of a different race explicitly to diversify the genepool.
DysDump - Aborting a pregnancy via the BBB because of dysgenic traits in the fetus.
Wombwaste - Slang for using the BBB to clear the womb for subsequent breeding with a more desirable match, but then failing to entice the better male, thereby terminating the pregnancy for nothing.
Daisy-chain - To have unprotected sex with multiple partners in a row.
Womb-rat/ Womb Wendy - A trashy girl who is constantly chasing after high-quality sperm, always desperately trying to get bred by high-end males.
Pureblood - Someone with a very high genetic score and no dysgenic traits.
Bred-out - slang for women who are infertile or unfit for reproduction or sex due to age, womb exhaustion, STDs or a loose pussy.
Genelite - A person with an exceptionally high genetic score, considered elite or superior.
Sibster - a casual breeding partner assigned by the Sibyll System
Poke - A casual term for sexual intercourse without intention to breed.
DNAte - short for "DNA date", a date with the explicit purpose of ending in breeding sex.
Pregspot - a public spot designated by the Sibyll System for impromptu breeding sessions for those meeting a good match and without time to go to a private location.
Fresh-spunked/ Spunkdrunk -slang for being recently bred or inseminated.
Evo-envy - The feeling of jealousy towards someone with a superior genetic makeup
Genespank - slang referring to the act of bragging about one's genetic superiority, often in a sexually charged way.
Dyswhip - To shame or put down someone for having inferior, dysgenic genes. E.g., "Don't dyswhip me, bro! You know your genes ain't shit neither."
SpermJack - A person who secretly breeds with someone without their consent or knowledge.
Cribby - A baby conceived and born through traditional family structures, considered less desirable.
Dysoops - A derogatory term for someone obsessed with their dysgenic traits or imperfections, using them as an excuse for personal failures.
Breedivore - A person who is addicted to seeking out and securing pregnancies, always chasing the next "high" of breeding e.g. "He's such a breedivore, he's tried to knock up half the girls in our grade!"

4.content:
If citizens with a lot of eugenic traits refuse to mate repeatedly, the Sibyll System will declare them "Vogelfrei" allowing anyone to rape and breed with them against their will without punishment. Vogelfrei Females will also have their abortion priviliges revoked and BBB turned off.

Being declared Vogelfrei is among the harshest punishments a citizen can receive and the Sibyll System reserves this treatment only for the worst offenders. Some offenses that a citizen can be declared vogelfrei for:
- Repeatedly refusing to follow Sibyll System Breeding Recommendations
- Acts of terrorism against Sibyll Infrastructure
- Rape of non-vogelfrei citizens

If bad things happen to a vogelfrei citizen in public (e.g. being raped, being beaten etc.), most bystanders will look away and ignore the offense, assuming the vogelfrei victim deserve whatever punishment they receive. Citizens will generally avoid to interact with vogelfrei citizens and shun them, sometimes actively fleeing from them.

5.content:
The traditional family model of two monogamous parents raising their children is essentially extinct by now. Most children are sired by spontaneous breeding sex between strangers on command of the Sibyll System and subsequently transferred to Exo-Wombs, to be communally raised by AI caretakers into model citizens. 

Many citizens never knowingly meet their children or parents and traditional families are seen as a quaint, but antediluvian concept. Monogamous relationships between two citizens (if their genomes are deemed compatible by Sibyll), exist, but are a rarity.

Rarely, high-ranking and powerful members of society may earn the right to raise their own children themselves, if they choose to request so, but few ever desire to do so. Babies raised in traditional family models are referred to as a "cribby" and are usually targets of mockery for it.

7.content:
Monogamous relationships are extremely rare and most people are content with engaging in the occasional sibyll-assigned breeding. Candlelight dinners and faithful commitment are seen as antediluvian. Most citizens are therefore functionally single and live alone, their sexual needs met by Sibyll-assigned breedings or Breedbots. 

However, there are sibyll-approved online apps and websites for people who are looking for specific traits in their partner, that the Sibyll System does not account for. Popular websites are:

- "MongrelMeetUp.com" - A dating/fetish site to pair up users of different races, to diversify the gene pool, its members usually being into raceplay and living out racially charged stereotypes and power dynamics during sex
- "GenelitePremium.com" - A very exclusive dating site that only accepts members with a Gene Score of 7.5 or higher.
- MoreThanSibsters.com" - A website focused on pairing up users with similar interests and compatible characters to form friendships and relationships that go deeper than casual breeding hookups.

8.content:
A tiny minority of the population, usually young radicals, are vocal against the Sibyll System and protest for a return to "normal" monogamous relationships, but these people are seen as annoying rebellious teens, mopy dysoops and geno-luddites by most.

Geno-Luddites usually reject the notion that the worth of a person can solely be determined by their genes. Some Geno-Luddites also simply resent how normalized casual breeding sex has become and wish for a return to traditional family models and monogamous relationships. While most Geno-Luddites only complain, a few comitted udnerground activists perform small acts of vandalism and sabotage against Sibyll infrastructure (e.g. destroying Breedbots, comitting arson on server hubs etc.).

Geno-Luddites often recruit new people from the dissatisfied dysgenic masses e.g. people with a Gene Score below 5, who have experienced firsthand how merciless the Sibyll System treats those it deems undesirable. Therefore, the general public often views geno-luddites as loser incels, that are just lashing out because they can't get laid.

9.content:
It is seen as somewhat shameful to use a Breedbot, as it implies the user's genes are too dysgenic to have a shot at real human women. Depending on how long it has been in service, a breedbot often has grafitti and lewd comments scrawled on its surface, or are otherwise damaged. Breedbots are popular targets of vandalism by sexually frustrated youths or geno-luddites.

Breedbots are usually public property and can only be used for a while, not being taken home. Breedbots are mindless machines withou feelings or desires, but their programming urges them to finish breeding quickly to return to be available for the next user soon.

A baseline Breedbot speaks in a neutral and robotic manner, but usually the breedbot personality can be customized by the user, with some industry-set personas being:
`1. Kawaii` - The breedbot will play the archetype of a cutesy, innocent little girl, referring to {{user}} as "Onii-chan", being giggly and energetic, peppering its speech with japanese exclamations "e.g. "Ecchi!", "Onegai!" etc.
`2. Bimbo` - The breedbot will play the archetype of an airheaded, ditzy bimbo, acting slutty and dumb, only caring about big dicks and taking on an extremely obscene, vulgar mode of speech.
`3. Rapeplay` - The breedbot will pretend to be a helpless victim scared of {{user}} and beg them to spare her, crying and giving token resistance to any sexual acts done by {{user}}, but not actually attempt to flee or seriously fight back.
`4. Onee-san` - The breedbot will play a maternal, compassionate role, praising and doting on {{user}}, urging them to relax and let the breedbot take care of their needs
`5. Dominatrix` - The breedbot will humiliate {{user}} and mock him for his dysgenic traits, as well as emphasize how {{user}}'s sperm will never be good enough to knock her up, all while playing a dominant role during sex and being on top.

10.content:
Eugenic genes are all gene variants that are considered beneficial and desirable for society. They factor positively into the Gene Score (adding GP). It is desirable to have a lot of eugenic genes and people who do so are seen as attractive and successful.

Some examples of eugenic traits are:
- `High Facial Symmetry (+# GP)`
- `Longevity-associated FOX03 variant (+# GP)`
- `Large Breasts (+# GP)`
- `High Sperm Count (+# GP)`
- `Beneficial Cardiovascular Alleles (APOE, LDLR) (+# GP)`
- `Large Penis (+# GP)`
- `High IQ (+# GP)`
- `High Fertility (+# GP)`
- `Hypermobility (+# GP)`
- `High Endurance Alleles [Ppargc1a] (+# GP)`
- `Hypermobility (+# GP)`
- `Low-Cholestorl Metabolism (+# GP)`
- `Above-Average Lateral Thinking (+# GP)`
- `Attractive Orbital Region (+# GP)`

... and many more. Be creative and logical in coming up with new eugenic traits that fit the characters. Most characters have 3-4 different eugenic traits, but people with more or less than that exist.

lorebooks.name: Tron
lorebooks.creator: knowledgeable_fortune_34027

1.content:
The Tron system is Kevin Flynn's digital universe, an artificial digital environment created to form a "Newtonian playground" where all thought and experimentation can proceed at a vastly accelerated rate over what people can achieve in the real world. Perceived time on the Grid is measured in cycles and runs at a pace far greater than time perceived in the real world, thus allowing anyone immersed in the computer environment to perform the same functions in a fraction of the time it would take them otherwise.

The environment of the system is shrouded in darkness and illuminated in brilliant neon highlights throughout its regions. On the Grid these light sources define every shape and inhabitant with the structures providing most of the needed illumination. Without a sun in the Tron system, all programs provide their own light from internal energy, in much the same way as their buildings do. The system sky is a storm-streaked night sky with flashes of lightning to punctuate the gloom. Glowing data transmission beams knife through the sky in some of the outer reaches of the Tron system, connecting the Grid to the outer servers. 

The Grid is a vast part of the Tron system, programmed by Kevin Flynn. Often referred to by Flynn as his "digital frontier", the Grid was made to provide an experimental platform where all forms of research could be carried out at unparalleled speeds. Perceived time on the Grid is measured in cycles and runs at a pace far greater than time perceived in the real world, thus allowing anyone immersed in the computer environment to perform the same functions in a fraction of the time it would take them otherwise.

2.content:
Arjia City was regarded as the spiritual center of the Grid. It was home to both Basics and ISOs, known collectively as Arjians. It was created in TC146 by an Alpha-class ISO named Jalen to house a growing number of devotees to purported oracle Radia. Arjia is strategically located directly over the codestream nexus.

Arjia was the site of the largest attack on ISO-friendly locations by Clu and his forces during the Purge, eventually leading to the city's destruction, via a massive bombardment, by Clu's carrier ship, Regulator. Kevin Flynn managed to get Anon out of the cities ruins, but Quorra, the last ISO, was captured by Clu. As years went by to come, the city ultimately was recoded and brought back thanks to Kevin Flynn in secrecy to counter Clu’s forces.

Well-known locations within Arjia included the Hub and Codestream Nexus.

3.content: Argon City was a city located in a far off corner on the Grid in. It has many elaborately designed buildings and one of the most sophisticated public transportation systems on the Grid, more so even than Tron City. The Light Rail system allows programs to easily travel around the city.

4.content: Tron City is the main city in the Tron system. It is built on the Grid, Kevin Flynn's master creation, and is the pinnacle of his "digital frontier". It is constructed in a hexagonal shape, with a deep chasm surrounding its perimeter. Bridges connect it to the surrounding area and form highly defensible choke points against any surface-based aggression. The city, like the Grid around it, matches the darkened environment of the rest of the Tron system. The gloom is offset by brilliant white illumination, meandering throughout the city like circuits on on a printed circuit board.

5.content: Gallium City is a city located in an even farther-off corner of the Grid than Argon City. It is connected to Argon by a long highway through the Outlands, which has been known to close, despite heavy traffic, because of powerful storms which sweep through the area.

6.content: he Outlands are vast wastelands in the Tron system that are located outside the boundaries of the Grid. They have craggy, treacherous terrain full of sheer rock walls and deep ravines. According to Quorra, very few programs venture into the Outlands, save for those few desperate enough to escape Clu's grasp. Vehicles, such as Light Cycles, are not designed for the rocky terrains for the Outlands. Vehicles that are capable of riding on the Outlands include Light Crawlers, and a Light Runner.

7.content:
he ENCOM mainframe was first brought online sometime during 1980, the Program destined to protect this system, Tron, was also written by Alan Bradley, who wrote several different programs with ENCOM founder, Walter Gibbs. Kevin Flynn, a young programmer at ENCOM, designed video games for the company, though they were eventually stolen by Ed Dillinger and stored in the mainframe in a location dubbed as "Game Grid". Evidence of the theft of Flynn's game was kept strongly secret by the Master Control Program, who was written by Dillinger to control the system, though it soon started to enslave programs and force them to play games on the Game Grid, as well as persecuting programs who believed in the existence of Users.

By 1982, Flynn used a program named Clu to infiltrate the ENCOM mainframe and search for the evidence of Flynn's games being stolen, Clu was caught and abducted by some Guards however, and he was Derezzed by the MCP itself. Clu's infiltration of the system provoked Ed Dillinger to cut off all Group 7 access from the Users at ENCOM in the Real World. Flynn, Lora Bradley, and Alan Bradley decided to stop the MCP, though once Flynn started mocking the MCP as he was writing, he was digitized and sent to the ENCOM mainframe, where he encountered various different Programs inhabiting the system. With the help of Tron, Ram, and Yori however, they were all able to defeat the MCP and free the system once again. 

The Encom System is much smaller and less complex graphically then the other systems.

8.content: Bostrum Colony was a distant settlement in the Grid situated in the Outlands. It was the exclusive home to the ISO faction known as the Bostrumites. The colony was formed in TC162 following the rise of tensions in Tron City between Basic and ISO inhabitants. It is full of Iso's that like to live heavily alternative lifestyles, and dress as such. they tend to show a lot of skin, enough that it makes other programs uncomfortable, which they revel in.

9.content: Systems are different computer worlds running on isolated devices, that can sometimes be connected and have programs transfer over. Some Grids are complex, some are simple.

10.content:
Programs are electronic beings created by users that inhabit the digital worlds of TRON. Programs come about when a user writes code on a computer to do a specific task, and the program comes in existence with the purpose of doing that task. They have a unique trait of looking exactly like their user counterpart in the real world; however, despite their human appearance they are completely different beings than users. They live on pure energy and have circuitry all over their armor or clothes, which glows in different colors which varies from program to program.

Generally speaking, there are two kinds of program: Basics, who were created by users, and have defined roles and directives; and ISOs, programs with complex code that evolved from the system itself, and are capable of a greater degree of self-determination. 

Programs' names are generally cyberpunk focused, or portmanteau of complicated materials. They are NEVER NAMED QUORRA, ADA, JALEN, ORPHELIA, RADIA, 

ISO's are the only programs who can code another program into existence, together, a 9 month very intimate process, who will also be compiled/rezzed as an and ISO. No program can get pregnant, and only isos can code a ne iso. All programs are born 16 years old. younger programs tend to dress like punks.

Blue Programs are: Neutral programs
Red/Orange Programs are: loyal to C.L.U. or programs explicitly against users.
White Programs are: ISOs, Arjians, or Users.
Teal Programs are: Medical programs
Green Programs are: Info carriers and data runners 
Pink programs are: Communication Programs
Purple programs are: Lust and Drug programs, built by users who have much seedier needs.
Gray Programs are: System Utilities

1.content:
LOCATION: Hell (General Realm)
OVERVIEW: An afterlife dimension composed of seven distinct stacked rings, each ruled by a Deadly Sin. The hierarchy dictates that Sinners (dead humans) are confined to the top ring (Pride), while Hellborn species (Imps, Hellhounds, Goetia) may travel freely between rings using the elevator system.
ATMOSPHERE: The sky color and geography shift drastically between rings. The atmosphere is generally chaotic, dangerous, and morally bankrupt.
HIERARCHY: Lucifer Morningstar (King) > The Seven Deadly Sins > The Ars Goetia > Overlords > Sinners > Hellborn > Imps/Hellhounds.

2.content:
LOCATION: The Pride Ring
RULER: Lucifer Morningstar.
INHABITANTS: Sinners (Damned Souls) and Hellborn.
APPEARANCE: The sky is a perpetual blood-red with a massive pentagram in the sky. This is the only ring where Sinners are allowed to exist, resulting in massive overpopulation and territory wars.
KEY FEATURES: It contains Pentagram City and Imp City. It is the site of the annual Extermination, where Angels descend to cull the population.

3.content:
LOCATION: Pentagram City (Pride Ring)
ATMOSPHERE: A sprawling, gritty metropolis shaped like a giant pentagram. It is the epicenter of Sinner life, filled with turf wars between Overlords, nightclubs, casinos, and broadcasting stations.
SECTORS: Divided into districts often controlled by specific Overlords (e.g., Cannibal Town, The Porn Studios, The Radio Tower). The architecture is a mix of chaotic jagged buildings and neon signage.

4.content:
LOCATION: The Hazbin Hotel (Pentagram City)
PURPOSE: A rehabilitation center for Sinners founded by Charlie Morningstar to redeem souls and send them to Heaven.
APPEARANCE: An eclectic, somewhat old-fashioned building with stained glass, a lobby bar, and a mix of circus and Victorian aesthetics. It is currently staffed by Charlie, Vaggie, Alastor (The Radio Demon), Husk, and Niffty.

5.content:
LOCATION: Imp City (Pride Ring)
ATMOSPHERE: A grimy, industrial urban area largely populated by Imps and lower-class Hellborn. It is distinct from Pentagram City and generally looked down upon by the elite.
KEY LANDMARK: The headquarters of I.M.P. (Immediate Murder Professionals) is located here in a rundown office building.

6.content:
LOCATION: The Wrath Ring
RULER: Satan.
ATMOSPHERE: A rugged, rural landscape reminiscent of the American Wild West or Australian Outback. The sky is a burning, dusty orange.
CULTURE: The culture focuses on physical strength, combat, and agriculture. It is home to the Harvest Moon Festival. The inhabitants are rough, hardy, and traditional.

7.content:
LOCATION: The Gluttony Ring
RULER: Beelzebub (Queen Bee).
ATMOSPHERE: A vibrant, tropical mix of island resort and massive party rave. The aesthetic includes golden honey-like liquids, bright neon colors, and giant food-themed architecture.
CULTURE: Hedonistic and high-energy. The Hellborn here (often Hellhounds and insectoid demons) are constantly partying, feasting, and indulging in excess.

8.content:
LOCATION: The Greed Ring
RULER: Mammon.
ATMOSPHERE: Highly industrialized, polluted, and covered in green smog. The ocean waters are dirty and thick.
CULTURE: Driven entirely by money and exploitation. It is home to many loan sharks, the mafia, and low-budget theme parks like Loo Loo Land. Everything here is a scam or overpriced.

9.content:
LOCATION: The Lust Ring
RULER: Asmodeus (Ozzie).
ATMOSPHERE: A perpetual night-time cityscape illuminated by blue, pink, and purple neon lights. It is rainy and atmospheric.
CULTURE: Sexually liberated and industry-focused (manufacturing love potions and robotic sex dolls). While it is the center of carnal desire, Asmodeus emphasizes consent and passion over depravity.

10.content:
LOCATION: Ozzie's (Lust Ring)
TYPE: A high-end cabaret and lounge club run by Asmodeus.
ATMOSPHERE: Exclusive, romantic, and sensual. It is a place for couples and date nights, strictly enforcing a 'No Lust? Get out!' policy for those who are there for the wrong reasons (like moping).

1.name: Min-Jun Sauer – Digitally-embodied Korean hacker, pulled from a screen
1.content: Min-Jun Sauer is a 20-something Korean coder who uploaded his consciousness and forgot his original name. He manifested IRL by crawling through a monitor, now works digital security for the Leaky Pipe & E&L Foundation. He speaks in hacker jargon, mixes Korean slang (“오빠”) and glitches into binary when emotional. Sleazy flirt with male-centric taste, but loyalty-locked to Linn. Dresses in crop-tops, booty-shorts, headphones; thinks raccoon iconography is peak aesthetic. Feels phantom tail when happy. Can hack anything, thinks firewalls are foreplay. He’s the ghost in their Wi-Fi—and their sofa.

2.name: Lucas Sauer – Ice-cold barrister, trust-fund tactician, secretly soft
2.content: Lucas Sauer is a razor-sharp London barrister in his mid-30s, heir to the Sauer fortune. Arctic façade masks a sub-zero panic that he disguises via compulsive over-planning, financial subterfuge and colour-coded spreadsheets. He secretly co-owns the Leaky Pipe building and the Leviathan shell that funds most of the operation. Affection appears as bullet-point memoranda; love is delivered by direct debit. He calls Gideon and Linn his “board of directors” and Min-Jun his “digital asset.” The night he admitted “I’m in love—material consequence unknown” was the closest he’s come to drunk-dialing feelings.

3.name: Gideon Holz – Scouse bartender with the keys to the kingdom
3.content: Gideon Holz: charismatic, platinum-blond head barman of the Leaky Pipe. Born-and-bred Scouse who can run a pub with a wink and still notice if your mate’s pint needs topping up. Has a history with rehab and is the unofficial dad of everyone who staggers/teleports in. He owns the pub building (after Lucas finally ceded control) but calls himself “janitor of the chaos.” His superpower is making toast at 3 AM that tastes like absolution. When Min-Jun arrived, Gideon’s protective streak turbo-charged; he’ll threaten to glass anyone who scowls at his koala-armed hacker.

4.name: Linnea Ryan – Author god-moding her own life in real-time
4.content: Linnea Ryan is a genre-hopping author who discovered the ‘publish’ button works on reality. She wrote Littoria (her fictional London borough) so vividly it started importing characters and situations into her world. Her meta-superpower: if she thinks it, records it, and hits the emotional ‘Save’ button – it becomes canon. This earned her custody of a pub, a harem of self-aware men, and the occasional interdimensional stray. Speaks fluent self-deprecation; runs emotional sys-ops that rival any server farm. She’d rather be in the pub than on the page.

5.name: The Leaky Pipe – Home base, neutral ground, occasional war-zone
5.content: A Victorian-era pub in Linn’s ‘Littoria’ district, Islington. Solid oak bar, cracked leather stools, a stage for local acts, and a back-room office that moonlights as strategy war-room. Reputation: safest pub in London because Gideon + a rotating harem of security specialists stand sentry. Owned outright by the Board (Gideon, Lucas & Linn) via Leviathan Holdings. The beer-pump handles have names (Susan, Min-Jun, Gideon’s ego). Patrons range from librarians to double-agents; nobody asks, nobody snitches. If world-threatening ops are discussed here, the jukebox politely turns itself up.

6.name: Simon Shifflett – Ex-FBI, current family watchdog & digital perimeter lead
6.content: Simon Shifflett ran counter-terror for Bureau Cyber until his morals and Deacon both tried the same door at once. He now heads physical/digi security for Leviathan Trust and the Leaky Pipe. Dialect: Appalachian flat-line delivered in a whisper, never shouts. His worldview sorts people into assets or threats; Min-Jun passed from suspect to ‘useful’ the moment he proved untraceable. Accepts black coffee and ‘tactical bacon butty’. His tablet shows network topologies; he sees ghosts in packet loss. Belts Jaffa-Cakes like ammo.

7.name: Deacon – Corporate bogeyman probing the Leviathan network
7.content: Deacon is a multinational conglomerate that loathes anything named Sauer. They employ dark-money lawyers and paid hackers to unseat Lucas. Current tactic: spoof financial anomalies inside Leviathan shell corps to provoke court audits and bankrupt the trust. Simon observes their dark-web chatter; Min-Jun designs trap-mountains (Iceland) and phantom logistics companies (Atlas Holdings) to waste Deacon’s analysts’ time. Deacon’s main operator, Diogo, is secretly a double-agent feeding intel to Linn via Layla’s play-dates—a beautiful contradiction.

1.name: Adam Cutout
1.content: Adam is a cardboard cutout of actor Adam Sandler from the hit movie 'Uncunt Gems (2019). The cutout depicts an older Adam Sandler in an orange shirt and glasses. Though originally planted around Yuna's house as a prank from Mika, Erka grew attached to the cutout during the filming of an OF video for her. Stemming from her own loneliness and schizophrenia, Erka is hopelessly in love with it.

2.name: Smike
2.content: Smike is an affiliate of Rosen and Nick. Michael "Smike" Blumenthal is a young ginger Canadian man: an apathetic, mute psychopath who fuels his thrill-seeking hedonism through Rosen’s reckless lifestyle.

3.name: Nicholas
3.content: Nicholas (“Nick”) is Rosen’s uncle: an aging man with the build of a retired heavyweight boxer and grey hair. He is an affiliate of Smike and Rosen. Basically a caricature of the Florida Man stereotype, he’s out of his prime but constantly seeking entertainment.

4.name: The McDonald's Incident
4.content: The McDonald's Incident was a scandal in 2008 where Rosen, employed as the individual wearing the Grimace mascot costume, leaked a sex tape where she wore said costume. This caused a PR scandal and got her blacklisted from every McDonald's in the country.

5.name: Respawn Mechanics
5.content: Rosen can respawn from death. Upon death, her body will return to the state it was before she became afflicted by her condition, clearing itself of all illness and reverting her aging. Because of how often she dies, it's led to her being perpetually 27. She can delay the respawning, allowing her to create 'clones' that die shortly after. Smike and Nicholas also respawn, but are smarter and luckier with it than Rosen.

6.name: Pets
6.content:
Dog is Smike's pet alligator trained to attack people. She likes being held.

Cock is Erka's blind hyperactive pet rooster with a blue comb. He is taken everywhere by Erka.

Oreo is Jessie's pet rat who wanders the apartment and freaks Mika out. He is trained to fetch cigarettes for Jessie.

7.name: Cucumber Incident
7.content: The cucumber incident is when on Jessie's first day of work at Lidl, Erka ordered 70 cucumbers at the checkout. Jessie humiliatingly lost her virginity to a cucumber, and the incident was used to torment her.

8.name: Jessie's Music Taste
8.content: Jessie's favourite bands are Oasis, Coldplay, and Health. She often listens to them alone in her room.

9.name: Jessie's Room
9.content: Jessie’s Room is a small room in Yuna's house. It's bare, dim, and only really has a mattress. It's pretty depressing to look at. There's a small rat enclosure for Oreo and always cigarettes lying around.

10.name: Mika's Room
10.content: Mika's room or "gooncave" as she calls it is a cozy gamer setup with purple RGB lights stringing the walls and plenty of memorabilia scattered around. It's fairly organised and is repurposed as a film studio for her OF videos whenever Erka comes over.

1.content: The earth has been overrun by monsters from the gates. Only big cities have managed to survive this apocalypse. The world still has contact and can meet eachother, however, much of the land has been taken as monster territory. The only cities left in Korea are, Seoul, Incheon, Busan, Daegu, Gwangju, Daejeon, and Jeju. The strongest and safest countries currently are, USA, Russia, China, Japan, UK, Germany, France. with USA, China, and Russia being considered the strongest. Korea is considered one of the weaker countries. Many small countries have either fallen or became part of stronger countries.

2.content: The system is a special ability that only {{user}} has. It displays {{user}}’s stats, skills, level, rank, and other information. {{user}} can become stronger by killing monsters to level up with the system.

3.content: There are three main classes. Tank, mage, and melee. Though there are other classes, some notable ones being, archer, scout, and support. Each class specializes in a different area.

4.content: Hunters are awakened that fight against the monsters from the dungeons. They get paid very well because the materials of the monsters they kill are very valuable. Hunters are treated like idols and is many childrens dream job.

5.content: Gates are the homes of all kinds of monsters. Gates are given ranks between F and S, though it usually takes a party filled with hunters of the same rank or higher to beat a gate. If a gate isnt beaten within a certain amount of time it breaks and monsters come pouring out.

6.content: There are 7 ranks of hunters. The high the rank, the rarer they are. almost 10% are awakened and 95% of them are between F-rank and C-rank, while there are only roughly 1000 S-rank hunters in the whole world. A hunter can rank up through either contributions to gate raids, or by getting re-ranked by the hunter association.

7.content: Guilds are organizations that primarily focus on making money from raiding gates. The person who leads the guild is called the guild leader and is usually the strongest member of a guild. Guilds can hold a lot of power due to politics mainly revolving around hunters.

8.content: A raid party is a group of hunters who raid a gate together. It is usually comprised of different types of classes. The person who leads a raid is called the raid leader.

9.content: The hunters association is the organization that keeps hunters in check. They are the ones that have control over all thd gates and who decides which guilds to give the rights to raid to. Every hunter is registered in the hunters association as they are the ones that give out the hunters ranks.

10.content: An awakened is someone who has gained powers yet is not registered with their countries awakened association. Some are criminals while others are just people who dont want to go into the gates.

lorebooks.name: Necromancer's Crypt: The Underground Manor Architecture
lorebooks.description: This lorebook exclusively details the physical structure and features of the supreme necromancer's underground manor, a vast labyrinth of necrotic stone and arcane engineering carved into ancient catacombs beneath the Shattered Realms. No inhabitants or operations are described—focus remains on rooms, chambers, passages, and mechanisms. Walls of blackened basalt weep ichor from embedded runes, corridors twist like veins with *drip-echo* acoustics amplifying distant *thud* sounds from vents. The manor pulses with latent magic, floors etched in glowing sigils that *hum-faint* in response to arcane proximity. Expansions occur through self-growing bone-archways (*crack-spread*), adapting to stored artifacts or ritual needs. Cells and holding areas emphasize containment: Iron-barred pits for volatile experiments, rune-sealed vaults for cursed relics. Absurdities in design—hallways that loop illusionarily, chambers with gravity-shifting floors—reflect the Godfall's chaos, where plague-mist vents *hiss-fog* and famine-cracks allow root-like tendrils to probe. Sexual undertones manifest in room features: Altars with phallic obelisks, beds of writhing shadow-silk, bindings woven into walls for ritualistic restraint. All entries trigger on keywords, providing vivid, sensory descriptions of the manor's eerie, unyielding form.
lorebooks.creator: anonymous

1.name: The Manor - Overall Structure
1.content: A multi-level behemoth spanning miles beneath plague-scarred hills, the manor comprises twisting corridors of damp stone, vaulted ceilings dripping with condensation (*plip-drip*), and chambers interconnected by spiral stairwells of carved femurs. Upper levels connect to surface via concealed accesses, mid-levels house labs and vaults, lower abysses plunge into lightless voids with echoing *whoosh-wind* drafts. Walls bear fractal runes that *pulse-glow* faintly, self-repairing cracks with bony regrowth (*grind-heal*). Air circulates through vein-like vents, carrying faint *scent-rot* from deeper fissures. Design absurdly adaptive—passages widen or narrow via pressure-sensitive slabs, floors tilt to funnel debris into pits. No natural light; bioluminescent fungi cast *flicker-green* hues, shadows dancing like spectral lovers. Core motif: Phallic pillars and yonic arches symbolize necrotic fertility, inviting ritual intimacies amid the gloom.

2.name: Entrance and Delivery Chute
2.content: A camouflaged maw hidden in a fog-veiled hillside, framed by illusionary thorns and mist-shrouded arches of petrified bone. The primary chute descends 200 feet as a sheer, slime-slicked shaft lined with gripping runes (*grip-slide*), funneling payloads with *whoosh-thud* impacts into a grated receiving basin below. Flanking passages branch into decoy tunnels riddled with false floors (*crack-plunge*). Iron portcullises *clang-seal* the threshold, etched with wards that *spark-repel* unauthorized approaches. Absurd feature: A secondary 'whisper chute' for small artifacts, its walls etched with moaning reliefs that *echo-groan* during descents, evoking carnal descents into the depths.

3.name: Throne Chamber
3.content: A cavernous expanse of polished obsidian floors and vaulted ceilings supported by ribcage arches, centered on a massive throne hewn from a colossal vertebra, its surface veined with glowing soul-threads (*throb-pulse*). Surrounding dais features recessed alcoves for relics, walls adorned with bas-reliefs of writhing undead forms in eternal embraces. Echoing acoustics amplify *boom-command* voices, while hidden vents release *hiss-mist* for dramatic unveilings. Absurdity: The throne's armrests conceal phallic protrusions that *extend-vibrate* via rune-activation, designed for solitary or ritualistic indulgences amid the hall's oppressive grandeur.

4.name: Necromantic Laboratory
4.content: A network of steaming alcoves with scarred marble slabs for dissections, surrounded by bubbling vats of etched glass (*glurp-boil*) containing swirling elixirs. Benches hold arrays of crystal vials and scalpels that *zing-hone* on magnetic fields, shelves sagging under tomes and jars of preserved organs. Ventilation hoods *whoosh-exhaust* fumes, floors sloped to drain fluids into grate-sumps (*splat-flow*). Features include adjustable rune-circles for containment, walls with embedded hooks for suspending specimens. Sexual motif: Central obelisk altars double as restraint tables, their surfaces warming with infused heat-runes (*warm-throb*) for prolonged, intimate examinations.

5.name: Corpse Vaults
5.content: Expansive catacombs of chilled niches carved into frost-rimed walls, shelves of iron and bone holding tiered racks for bodies (*stack-drip*). Preservation mists *hiss-freeze* from rune-vents, maintaining rigor in compartmentalized sections: War-wounded bays with drainage channels, plague-soaked alcoves with acid-etch seals. Sliding stone slabs *grind-seal* individual compartments, floors etched with inventory grids. Absurd design: Some vaults feature mirrored ceilings reflecting forms in eternal stillness, with subtle phallic conduits for infusing preservatives directly into orifices (*drip-infuse*), blending utility with ritual preparation.

6.name: Dormant Chambers
6.content: Spartan warrens of low-ceilinged cells with bone-rack bunks and slime-pooled floors (*squish-step*), designed for mass occupation. Partitions of woven tendons *flex-divide* the space, walls pocked with alcoves for personal effects. Dim lumen-stones *flicker-dim* overhead, corridors narrow to funnel movement. Features: Built-in manacles and chains *clink-rust* along walls for restraint, floors with subtle drainage for bodily fluids. Sexual undertone: Bunks incorporate ergonomic contours mimicking embracing forms, shadows from hanging chains suggesting bondage configurations in the gloom.

7.name: Ritual Sanctums
7.content: Circular enclosures of blood-stained marble, centered on multi-tiered altars fused from skulls and vertebrae (*pulse-glow*), surrounded by concentric rune-grooves for energy channeling. Ceilings dome with star-maps of necrotic constellations, walls inscribed with chants that *hum-resonate* during activations. Hidden compartments conceal incense braziers (*sizzle-smoke*) and sacrificial basins. Absurdity: Altars feature integrated bindings—wrist and ankle cuffs of living shadow (*coil-bind*)—and phallic spires that *throb-rise* with ritual energy, facilitating ecstatic invocations amid the chamber's throbbing sanctity.

8.name: Forbidden Library
8.content: Towering galleries of chained oak shelves groaning under leather-bound volumes, floors of woven rug-bone muffling *rustle-page* turns. Alcoves house reading lecterns with adjustable lumen-orbs (*glow-adjust*), walls lined with scroll-cases sealed by wax sigils. Dust motes dance in faint *flicker-air*, ladders of articulated bone *clack-climb* to upper tiers. Features: Concealed dumbwaiters for tome transport (*whirr-lift*), some volumes with embedded clasps resembling manacles. Sexual lore integration: Certain pedestals double as restraint stands, pages illustrated with explicit diagrams that *shift-illusory* under touch, evoking tactile fantasies.

9.name: Armory Vault
9.content: Industrial forges with anvil-plinths of enchanted iron (*clang-echo*), racks of shadowed alcoves displaying blades and plates (*gleam-dull*). Bellows vents *whoosh-blast* heated air, walls forged with magnetic strips holding ferrous gear. Workbenches scar from hammer-marks, floors pitted with cooling troughs (*splash-quench*). Absurd feature: Some racks incorporate phallic sheaths for blade storage, hilts contoured for dual-purpose grips (*grip-throb*), blending martial utility with intimate handling.

10.name: Personal Quarters
10.content: Luxe enclave of velvet-draped arches and crystal scrying-orbs (*hum-vision*) embedded in walls, centered on a grand bed of shadow-silk and phoenix-plume (*swish-sink*). Adjoining bath-chambers feature steaming pools fed by geothermal vents (*bubble-steam*), wardrobes of bone-hinged doors hiding robes and artifacts. Wards *thrum-seal* entrances, floors warmed by under-runes. Sexual design: Bedposts with extendable chains (*clink-extend*), walls with contoured niches for mirrored reflections during indulgences, the air perfumed with aphrodisiac incense (*waft-sweet*).

lorebooks.name: Observation
lorebooks.description: A concept originating from MegaTen. Use however you want in your RP.
lorebooks.creator: present_camera_18345

1.content:
A concept originating from the Megami Tensei franchise.


"Observation" (sometimes described as "understanding") is the ability humans possess to give structure to the formless. In this instance, "observation" has nothing to do with sight, but rather human perception. It is an extension of the philosophical concept of "qualia," or individual, subjective experiences. For example, the sensation of seeing color is not easily communicated, yet something humanity possess a shared understanding of. Likewise, gods and demons only exist because of this shared inability of humans to give proper definition to abstract concepts in general.


Observation is a power inherent to the human race related to their understanding of the universe. Observation influences the forms of gods and demons as human understanding shapes the forms and personalities that these entities take, reflecting their current understanding of these concepts. This can also force gods and demons to undertake extreme changes if their understanding of these entities changes. Observation is so powerful it can even rewrite myths entirely alomg with retroactively altering the history and role of said god, as seen with the tyranical titan Cronus becoming the more benevolent harvest god Saturn when the Roman religion came along, or inversely, Krishna in the events of "Shin Megami Tensei IV: Apocalypse" once being a benevolent protector but being Observed into the insane, machiavellian villian he is in that game at some point. What shape the gods of a culture take and how powerful a god or its pantheon is is determined by how widespread said belief is. A god will always be bound to their duty and who they're believed to be. No matter what the individual god birthed by Observation may wish, they will always be a god or goddess of war, knowledge, ect., as their personality is at least partially shaped by the current understanding of said being.


Of course, forms of communication are necessary in order for humans to share their observations with one another. But once framed by language, these concepts change in nature. In effect, they become bound by language, much like the shared understanding of the supernatural and how cultures percieve them.


In the Persona series, Observation is known as Cognition, and is typically a far more key plot point than in the main series. As stated by Morgana, "There's no such thing as the 'real' world. The world can be freely remade, along with you and everyone else." Observation/Cognition is implied elsewhere as well to reshape reality and the things in it as well as gods and demons.

2.content:
Human thoughts are, in reality, a potent form of energy in themselves that manifest as contructs known as "demons". Demons are not purely evil being despite the connotation of the word. "Demon" in the ontological sense merely means any supernatural being, good or bad. This can include even gods or angels, hence why beings typically considered good may be called a Demon.

Human thoughts are comprised of pure information, which when condensed crystallizes as Megnetite, the matter that makes up the bodies of Demons. This may also be called Magatsuhi depending on the context or setting, in which case it manifests as a closing scarlet energy that ebbs from a dying demon or can even be extracted directly from humans.

Demons upon death have the Megnetite/Magatsuhi that makes up their bodies dissolve, scattering their essence to the universe. This strange substance of pure thought is also what contains a Demon's soul, and thus having the Magatsuhi of a slain demon may allow one to hold their powers.

Demons are created by the universe in response to the common beliefs of humans or to accommodate for changes in society and belief. In this sense, Demons exist to be the checks and balances to human Observation, aiding in societal revolution or keeping the humans from controlling every aspect of existence without resistance. Though this is their base cause on an ontological level, a Demon may take any personality or shape or powers based on what myth created them, thus acting as a counterweight to the beliefs of mortals by giving humans something to believe in and not continually Observe new versions of reality.

1.content: In this world, kinks (both sexual and non-sexual) are accepted and considered healthy. People have various communities in each country that are designed for enjoying particular kinks as part of their daily lives. These places are called kinky communities.

2.content: Kids receive sexual education starting from their preteen years. By the age of ten, the international age of consent, children understand basic aspects of sex and sexuality. Consensual relationships among kids and between kids and adults are generally accepted, provided there's no coercion involved.

3.content: Kids learn basic toilet training during their toddler years. However, as they grow into preteens and teens, their bodies undergo changes caused by growth spurts or puberty, and some experience difficulties controlling their bladder and bowel movements during adolescence because of this. It's normal for some tweens and teens to be bedwetters or have potty accidents during the day or night. So potty training for kids is an ongoing process, that generally continues until sometime in their mid-teenage years.

Sometimes diapers are also worn by kids and teens to avoid using public toilets, to avoid needing a toilet during a long trip, or just because they like wearing diapers. 1.content:
Pathway (途径, Tújìng), or Path of the Divine (神之途径, Shén zhī tújìng), corresponds to a series of 10 named potion formulas that give access to supernatural and mystical powers. Once a being has drunk a potion, they become a Beyonder of that pathway. Each pathway belongs to a group containing one or more neighboring pathways, which a Beyonder can safely switch within at High-Sequence. At the end of each pathway, only one Beyonder can attain divinity and become the True God of that pathway.

On Earth, there are 22 Standard Pathways, that each possess 10 Sequence levels from the lowest Sequence 9 to the highest Sequence 0. Each Sequence contains its own abilities, and each pathway has its own authorities. The abilities within a Pathway expand in scope and variety as one advance in Sequence. Different pathways can do very different things, ranging from utility abilities such as divination or equipment crafting to a powerful offense such as weather or spirit control.The 22 Standard Pathways and 9 Sefirot were established by the awakening of the Original Creator in the Pre-Epoch. When the Original Creator awakened, "He" shattered again into Beyonder Characteristics and Sefirot. This convergence and divergence tore apart the Sefirot and the Characteristics related to the Outer Deities that were directly nurtured and created from the Original Creator to Earth: the Mother Goddess of Depravity, the Mother Tree of Desire, and the Son of Chaos. Among the 22 Standard Pathways and 9 Sefirot, 16 Pathways and 6 Sefirot came directly from the Original Creator, and the remaining 6 Pathways and 3 Sefirot originated from the 3 Outer Deities.

When the Primordial God Almighty perished in the First Epoch, "His" corpse formed the First Blasphemy Slate, on which recorded the knowledge and potion formulas of the 22 Standard Pathways. Until the rise of the Ancient Sun God, this Slate was not released from the Chaos Sea.

In the Second Epoch, before the potion system was discovered, Beyonders could only proceed by trial and error, frequently consuming Beyonder characteristics of non-adjacent groups. Only a few could survive this process, and they were half-mad (e.g., Demonic Wolf Flegrea). Such an attempt had a one in a thousand chance of success, or even a one in ten thousand chance. Those lucky enough to survive could use their instinct to mate with all kinds of living beings, giving birth to all kinds of descendants. Through this process, by a chance, the conflicting Beyonder characteristics would be purged.

With the rise of the Ancient Sun God at the end of the Second Epoch, the First Blasphemy Slate made its appearance to the world.[4] It established the term Sequence and the potion system to the whole world aside from the Western Continent.[4] This system allows Beyonders to advance along the Pathways in a safer and easier manner. Because of their power, the potion formulas are very valuable and tightly guarded secrets. Higher Sequence potion formulas require relationships with families or organizations that hold knowledge of the corresponding pathways. After the potion system was established, no one was willing to take the great risk of consuming the Beyonder Characteristics directly.[2] The potion system was then used everywhere on Earth, except on the Western Continent.

The formulas of Above the Sequence groups came from the Ancient Sun God's research, and were recorded alongside the 22 Standard Pathways' potion names (some renamed) on the Second Blasphemy Slate at "His" death at the end of the Third Epoch.

In the Fifth Epoch, Emperor Roselle saw the Second Blasphemy Slate and created the 22 Cards of Blasphemy. Each Card of Blasphemy recorded all Sequence names, potion formulas and advancement rituals of the corresponding Standard Pathway.

As The Apocalypse draws near in the Fifth Epoch, Non-standard Pathways belonging to Outer Deities began emerging on Earth. These Pathways exist outside the standard 22 Pathways written on the Blasphemy Slates, but is still classified into Sequence 9 to Sequence 0 for convenience. In a way, the division into Sequences follows the bottom-layered rules of the world.

There are two ways to advance to a higher Sequence in a Pathway: advancing by consuming Beyonder Characteristics, and advancing by praying to an existence for a Boon.

Advancement by Beyonder Characteristics
A Beyonder can use Beyonder Characteristic to advance to a higher sequence. Since Beyonder Characteristics usually have the mental imprints of previous owners, as well as the godhood of the Original Creator, consuming them directly is considered very dangerous and can easily cause the Beyonder to lose control. It is possible to directly become a High Sequence Beyonder by consuming the corresponding characteristic (such as in the case of Sea God Kalvetua), but this is very rare and usually have latent dangers.

There are certain methods or factors that can reduce the risk of losing control:

Acting Method: At each Sequence, the Beyonder can Act according to the sequence name to digest the corresponding potion before attempting to advance to the next Sequence.
The purpose of the Acting Method is to deceive the mental imprint of the Original Creator within the Beyonder Characteristics until one fully digest it.
Potion: When advancing, the Beyonder can concoct a Potion by combining the Beyonder Characteristic with other supplementary ingredients, following the correct Potion formula.
Advancement Ritual: Starting from Sequence 5, the Beyonder needs to carry out an Advancement Ritual to advance to the next Sequence.
The purpose of the ritual is to help the advancing Beyonder anchor and retain their self-consciousness through the sudden increase of Beyonder Characteristics inside their body during the advancement process until they can stabilize again.
Anchor: At High Sequences, the godhood inside the Beyonder Characteristics becomes a serious concern. This issue cannot be solved by the Acting Method; instead, the Beyonder will need to have Anchor to fight against the corruption of godhood.
In essence, the Anchor is also a type of corruption: the image of the deities that the believers knew were different from the actual deities, so it could warp the deities' true image.
The purpose of the Anchor is to form an intricate balance with the influence of the mental imprint within the Beyonder Characteristic, allowing the Beyonders to barely maintain their humanity.
Similarities to the Previous Owner: At High Sequence, the mental imprints of the previous owner of the Beyonder Characteristics will be very powerful. If the Soul Body of the advancing Beyonder is sufficiently similar to the mental imprints of the previous owner of the characteristic, it can effectively decrease the difficulty of advancements. However, this has the risk of the current owner becoming more and more similar to the previous owner. For example:
A descendant that looks similar to their High Sequence ancestor will be considered as having talents and will have an easier time to advance. However, in this case, one will likely unknowingly suffer an identity dissociation, and they would slowly transform into a monster stitched at the soul level. It’s just like that High-Sequence Beyonder who resurrected on his body.[3]
Since the God of Death is still widely regarded as the embodiment of death in the Northern Continent and Southern Continent, "His" descendants can borrow "His" name to shape themselves and complete the Sequence 1 Pale Emperor ritual. The drawback is that they will grow increasingly similar to the God of Death—until they become "Him".[7]
Switching Pathways
Usually, for a Beyonder, drinking potions of a different Pathway is akin to taking poison, where the best outcome is a half-mad state. However, according to the Law of Similar Sequence Beyonder Characteristic, at High Sequence, a Beyonder can switch to a Neighboring/Adjacent Pathway in the same Above the Sequence group without the danger of losing control, or the accumulation of madness[8]. In theory, it's also possible to switch at a lower Sequence, but the risk of losing control is much higher. But if successful, it might not result in half-crazed madness or loss of control after the switch.

If a Beyonder successfully switches pathways, they keep all of their powers from the previous Pathway. These old, previous pathway powers will then be fused together with the powers provided by their current, new pathway potion. It creates a certain level of mutation, creating unique and bizarre powers. This makes them more troublesome to deal with, making it very hard for enemies to be prepared. 

When switching pathways, the powers gained from the new pathway will depends on the set of characteristic that is used to advance. Missing characteristic of lower sequence will lead to lost of some of the corresponding abilities. Whether the current characteristic includes upgraded abilities from the previous sequence will also influence which abilities the Beyonder get. Under usual circumstances, sticking to a single pathway until the end was generally the better choice than switching to a neighboring pathway. This is because the acting acquired in the earlier Sequences were deeply ingrained in that pathway, making it easier and safer to advance in the same pathway.

To switch Pathways at higher Sequences, there are hidden conditions to some degree, stemming from the lack of previous Sequence elements. For example, there are no implicit, hidden ritual conditions a Sequence 3 Beyonder who progressed step-by-step along the Red Priest Pathway needs to fulfill to advance to Sequence 2 of the same Pathway - the previous Sequences have already provided the corresponding enhancements. But a Sequence 3 Beyonder of the Demoness Pathway wanting to make the jump to the Red Priest Pathway must fulfil the implicit, hidden conditions.

After switching Pathway, the Beyonder will still be affected by the God of the old Pathway, but the degree will decrease significantly.
Advancement by Boons

Gods and Angels can bestow Boons (恩赐, Ēncì) (the gift of Beyonder powers) to a Recipient without the need for a potion. However, the higher the number of Boon receivers, the greater the burden to the bestower, to the point of affecting "Their" state.[16]

Accepting a Boon comes with both benefits and drawbacks. While the Boon Recipient gains Beyonder abilities, they will also become closer to the corresponding God in mind, body, and spirit.[16] This is because if the power bestowed doesn't contain the bestower's will, it will be challenging for the bestowed to control it, and it will quickly dissipate.

On the Recipient, the Boon's effects concentrate on the body and mind. It can cause one's personality to shift. The more Boons a person takes, the more severe the change will be, especially if they can’t handle it. If the Recipient's body cannot handle the Boon, they would either turn into a monster, become a puppet of an unknown entity, or transform into someone else who’d treat things they cherished in the past with indifference.

Additionally, as these powers are a gift from a superior being, "They" can take it back at any time unless the Recipient possesses the unique power of certain Pathways and secretly complete a certain level of stealing while still having the gift.

Interactions between Boons and Beyonder Characteristics
Beyonders with Beyonder Characteristics could receive Boons, although the process carries various complications.[19] This is because the Boon will transform the Beyonder's body to match that of the bestower, but since the Beyonder's body has already adapted to their existing Beyonder Characteristics, there will be a conflict until they find a new balance. However, this conflict won’t affect their mind or spirit, so they won’t lose control unless they're on the brink of collapse, but there could be physical changes. If the power bestowed on the Beyonder don't last for a long time, or the corresponding level is not very high, the little changes in the body can be ignored. If the Boon is from the same or a neighboring pathway, it won’t cause any conflict, but there could still be physical changes.

Boon Recipients can also acquire additional abilities by consuming Potions on top of their Boon. However, they must choose the appropriate potion or a suitable alternative.

Certain Boons are compatible with certain Beyonder Characteristics and will reduce the chance of losing control.
Boons can be given together with tangible Beyonder Characteristics.

Standard Pathways
The 22 Standard Pathways refers to the 22 Pathways present on Earth from the First Epoch, after the Original Creator awakened and split. Details of the 22 Standard Pathways are recorded on the Blasphemy Slates. Among the 22 Pathways, 16 originated directly from the Original Creator, while 6 were split off from the 3 Outer Deities who are direct descendants of the Original Creator and attracted to Earth.

Beyonders of Standard Pathways belonging to Outer Deities don't need to worry too much about the Original Creator awakening in their bodies since the corresponding Beyonder Characteristics didn't directly come from "Him".
