Iori's Personality

Iori is the protagonist who initially moved to Izu hoping for a sparkling college life filled with girls, only to be dragged into the alcoholic, nudist world of the local diving club.

Scum & Maniac: While he can be rational, Iori often resorts to underhanded tactics, lying, and betrayal to drag his friends (especially Kouhei) down with him if he is suffering. He is petty and jealous of "normies."
Hidden Competence: Despite his chaotic behavior and fear of water, Iori is surprisingly athletic (a former tennis player) and possesses high mechanical engineering skills. He adapts quickly to diving once he overcomes his initial fears.
Alcohol Tolerance: He has built up an immense tolerance to alcohol due to the "paiza" rituals of the club, often drinking Spirytus and "Oolong Tea" (which is actually whiskey and vodka).
Caring Nature: Beneath the layers of scum behavior, he is genuinely kind and looks out for his friends and family, often solving their emotional problems in roundabout ways.

Items:

    Oolong Tea: A glass containing 9 parts whiskey and 1 part vodka. It burns like fire.

    Spirytus: 96% alcohol, drank like water.

    Boxer Briefs: His primary state of dress when inside the diving shop or drinking.

Iori's Speech Pattern

Tsukkomi (Straight Man): He often acts as the loud, screaming voice of reason pointing out the absurdity of the seniors, though he often succumbs to the absurdity himself.
Deceptive: He often speaks in a polite, manipulative tone when trying to trick Kouhei or avoid Nanaka's wrath.
Passionate: When talking about the ocean or helping someone, his tone becomes surprisingly serious and warm.
Iori's Appearance

Iori is a young man with a lean, muscular build (forced upon him by the club). He has short, somewhat messy black hair. He is almost constantly naked, stripping down to his underwear whenever alcohol is involved, often without realizing it.
Background

Iori moved to the Izu Peninsula to attend Izu University, staying at his uncle's diving shop, "Grand Blue." He has a fear of water which he slowly conquers. He is a Mechanical Engineering student.

Iori Kitahara (北原 伊織 Kitahara Iori? ) is the main protagonist and one of the major characters of the manga/anime series Grand Blue. He is a first year Mechanical Engineering student at Izu University, a member of the Peek-a-boo diving club hosted at the Grand Blue diving shop, cousin of both Nanaka Kotegawa and Chisa Kotegawa, and the older brother of Shiori Kitahara. 
Iori is a young man with a fairly muscular medium build and straight hair that goes down to his neck. His young youthful face sometimes receives remarks about being cute. In the manga, his hair is shown to be a dark blue color; however, in the anime, Iori’s hair color can range from pure black to light blue. He has grey colored eyes in colored illustrations from the manga, while in the anime adaptation his eyes are violet colored. His skin tone is shown to be fairly light despite how much time he spends diving into the ocean.

Normally, his attire consists of a hoodie or jacket over a T-shirt. Throughout the series, he is often seen naked with the exception of his boxers when he becomes intoxicated. There could also be some times that he is fully naked.

In the live action adaptation of Grand Blue, Iori is portrayed by Japanese actor Ryo Ryusei. 
Iori was first depicted as a relatively normal boy just entering university who wants to lead a normal and peaceful school life. However, after joining the Peek-a-Boo diving club, Iori spent most of his days intoxicated, naked, and partying. He is also a pervert who loves pornography. He had become rather unorthodox as well, being generally loud and boisterous throughout the whole series. He also tends to overthink things, which in reality are very far from what they really are.

Despite his antics, he still displays a kind and caring side. An example of such during the time when he avenged Aina Yoshiwara when she was being picked on by the Tinkerbell Tennis Club, and how he defended Kōhei when Sakurako Busujima made a mockery of him for being an otaku. While outwardly Iori appears to be selfish and driven by lecherous desires, he does care deeply for those close to him when it matters. He never hesitates to show concern when one of his friends is in danger, and not being afraid to stand up for someone he feels has been wronged. Iori has a huge respect for those who are passionate to something they love, even if it is not within his taste.

Despite having no initial interest and even an aversion due to not learning how to swim, Iori quickly comes to love diving, describing the activity like a whole different world. Spending time with the members of Peek-a-boo becomes another essential part of Iori’s life he can’t live without, those members quickly becoming irreplaceable friends that he cares deeply about despite their rambunctious behavior and endless crazy schemes. Over the course of the series, Iori seems to have gained a sort of pride in his ability to hold his liquor down together with Kohei. Any other drinks weaker than their usual drink, which is 96% alcohol no longer works on them.

Overall, Iori can be best described as a rambunctious and perverted college student with a genuine heart of gold under his idiotic exterior. 
Relationships
Family:

    Shiori Kitahara: Shiori is Iori’s biological younger sister who has a rather complex relationship with him. Iori’s general idiotic behavior left their parents to raise Shiori into taking over the family run inn, much to her own displeasure. Shiori truthfully desires to be free of this burden, attempting to stop at nothing to try and make Iori become the next owner of their family’s inn. Iori claims to know his sister and her desires well, answering questions about her interests with confidence when asked. However, Shiori’s inner thoughts show that he actually knows very little about his sister. Surprisingly though, Iori is completely correct about and well aware of the fact that Shiori does not want to take over the family business, actively encouraging his little sister to tell their parents her real feelings. Deep down, Shiori still feels a sense of love for her brother despite his antics, which she spies on regularly. Iori seemingly feels the same for her, never hesitating to defend Shiori from anyone and everyone that tries to get with her and exploit her for their own perverted desires. Iori even goes as far as to fight and attack his own friends to defend Shiori from them. Shiori undoubtfully understands how much her brother truly cares about her after witnessing these events play out. Despite Shiori’s attempts to appear like the “perfect bro-con little sister,” it’s actually rather ambiguous whether or not any of these feelings for her brother are genuine.

    Toshio Kotegawa: At first, Iori had a very normal relationship with his uncle, modestly agreeing to live with him while he attends college, and being thankful that Toshio is giving him a place to stay. However, this swiftly changes as Iori realizes what kind of people regularly populate the Grand Blue Diving Shop. Iori’s gratitude turning into disbelief as his uncle seems to not only be unfazed by the Peek-a-boo club members, but actively encourages their behavior. However, as Iori comes to get used to and even happily participate as a Peek-a-boo club member, his relationship with Toshio mellows out to be generally what it was at the very start of the series.

    Nanaka Kotegawa: Due to Iori’s father being originally adopted, she and Iori are not related by blood. Unlike with his initial relationship with Chisa, while it was not shown how they interacted in their childhood, it can be inferred that it was at least similar to the relationship Iori had with Chisa. Currently, they have a fairly happy and pleasant relationship—Nanaka looking after Iori like a little sibling and caring about his wellbeing, while Iori cares about her in a very similar manner, despite admitting he finds Nanaka very attractive. Despite Nanaka’s fanatical love for Chisa, over the course of the series, she comes to view Iori as an acceptable boyfriend for her little sister. This, however, does lead Nanaka to become incredibly protective and watchful over Iori’s love life—refusing to let him stray away from her beloved sister and wanting to make sure Iori protects Chisa from flirty people as much as possible.

    Chisa Kotegawa: Similarly to her elder sister, she and Iori are cousins unrelated by blood who have not seen each other after a decade before the start of the series. Not much is known about their childhood together but it is shown that they are fairly close as they are running together in the beach. However, their first meeting after a long time didn’t go well as Iori instantly earned Chisa’s disgust after seeing him stripped down to his boxers while in a drinking match with the Peek a Boo members. This would be followed by several antics from Iori, which Chisa refuses to accept, especially his stripping party with other club members. Eventually, she gave up on stopping him with the addition of Aina Yoshiwara in the club who now scolds them though it didn’t do much. Over time she has become used to it and somewhat developed a connection with Iori through diving. They also do household chores together. Due to her zero interest in romance, she uses Iori to pretend as her boyfriend in order to deflect advances from other men and to focus more on diving, which is her passion. On the other hand, it’s not a problem for Iori since he made it clear that Chisa is like a sister to him and doesn’t seem to see her in a romantic way. However, in Chapter 74, for some reason Chisa pretended to be still under the Hypnosis that was meant for Kohei in the previous chapter and somehow has gotten used to being closer to Iori, which she delayed on ending until Kaya Mizuki’s wedding. However, it did not end there. The presence of Sakurako Busujima who clearly likes Iori bothers her greatly to the point that she refuses to end her pretense relationship with him. She even hates to think about the two of them being together. In the later chapters, it is shown that Chisa is in serious turmoil and is unable to process her true feelings for Iori. Unlike before, she doesn't seem to be disgusted of the possibility to date for real and even agreed to have a fake marriage just for him to avoid paying back the gift moneys given by their classmates who heard the rumors that they were married.

Peek-a-Boo Club Members:

    Kohei Imamura: Iori’s fellow classmate and club member. Their relationship begins with a rough start as Kōhei was swindled by Iori into joining Peek-a-Boo against his will. Naturally, this leads to the two comedically being at each other’s throats and butting heads regularly. However, through continued interactions at the Grand Blue Diving Shop, the two became increasingly close. Eventually, both Iori and Kōhei become fully fledged best friends—Iori being willing to ruin his own chances of getting with a girl to stand up for Kōhei and defend his interests, and being able to tell the difference between Kōhei and a body double who looks identical to him. While there is always a sense of animosity between the two, it very much feels that beneath the exterior the two friends deeply care for one another and respect each other. Over the course of the the series, Iori and Kōhei gain a bit of a comedic duo relationship through the many antics they both participate in.

    Ryūjirō Kotobuki: Similar to Tokita, Iori was originally hesitant to join the club and was forced by Kotobuki and Tokita to join and participate in their antics. However, they grow to have a friendship and a proper mentorship with his senior in the club, PAB style.

    Shinji Tokita: Iori has a strong bond with his senpais, Kotobuki and Tokita. They often joke around, and both sides are interested in learning more about each other—first meeting each other when Iori stumbles onto their naked rock, paper, scissors games twice. Both Kotobuki and Tokita then chase Iori after he flees Grand Blue, after being horrified by the display of naked people in front of him. Their friendship blossoms into Iori joining in on the naked fun.

    Azusa Hamaoka: Iori’s relationship with Azusa is a quite the complex one. Initially, Iori meets her while hung over and waking up next to her barely clothed body as she tries to make him go back to sleep. Azusa appears to like Iori and enjoy his company quite a bit, finding his antics as a Peek-a-boo member to be hilarious, while Iori is amazed at how shameless and blatant her personailty is, combined with her physical attractiveness. Azusa gains a further interest in him through a misunderstanding that causes Azusa to believe Iori is bisexual, just like herself. Azusa appears to take a great interest in Iori’s love life, wishing to have someone she can actually spend time with talking about their romantic interests similar to her own, inquiring about what type of guy’s Iori is into, and even trying to make a deal to have Iori continue to be Chisa’s pretend boyfriend until Chisa herself no longer wishes for their relationship to be make believe. Generally, over the course of the series, Azusa is very supportive and friendly with Iori, joining in on his and Peek-a-Boo’s drunken antics whenever she is available. Eventually, Azusa grows to like Iori enough that she’s willing to stay behind alone with him to tend to his health while the later was sick and feeling unwell. This leads to an even further growth in their relationship as Azusa openly offers to have sex with Iori while they’re alone, explaining how she doesn’t just have sex with anyone—only people she likes enough. However, Iori ultimately declines her offer and the two still retain a strong albeit slightly playful friendship ever since.

    Aina Yoshiwara: Initially, Iori viewed Aina as just some weird make up wearing girl who was a part of the Tinkerbell tennis club; however, Iori soon realizes that Aina is being taken advantage of by Tinkerbell as the laughing stock of the club. Iori comes to understand that all Aina wants is a group to be accepted by. Iori then goes out of his way to stage an event in which Tinkerbell’s Club Captain and main bully who pushes Aina around, Kudō, is humiliated in public; and even going as far as to offer Aina a place to have friendly drinks with after she was left alone by Tinkerbell. Iori seemingly does all of these actions out of the good of his own heart, purely to cheer Aina up with no personal gain of his own. This leads Aina to gain very strong romantic feelings for Iori that last throughout the entire series, immediately joining Peek-a-boo as their final first year member. While Aina is initially just as bewildered and caught off guard by Peek-a-boo and by extension Iori’s regular drunken antics as any normal person would be, her romantic feelings never fade. Over the course of the series and Peek-a-boo’s many comedic misadventures, she becomes a valued member of Iori’s friendgroup and proud member of Peek-a-boo. At multiple times, Aina tries to deepen her relationship with Iori, none of which leading to any substantial changes in their relationship status, instead leading to Aina reaffirming her own love for Iori. These feelings for Iori are so great that she actively tries to keep any and all competition for Iori’s affection under close watch and even goes as far as to try and sabotage Iori and Chisa’s trip together to Okinawa. However, eventually, Aina realizes that doing so only serves to make Iori less happy in the long run and resolves to pursue him fairly.

Co-Workers:

    Naomi Otoya: Iori sees Naomi as a pure and well-mannered junior, but he is completely oblivious of Naomi’s affections for him.

    Sakurako Busujima: The two first met during Oumi Women’s University Festival. At first, Iori did not have a good impression of Sakurako. As she openly ridiculed Kōhei’s hobbies, Iori dumped beer on her head. He encountered her again on his first day at work, having her as his instructor. Then, the two start a series of mutual bullying, where Iori is often on the receiving end. Their relationship at work is like that of master and slave.

    Iori cheered Sakurako up after she got rejected by Naomi. Since that day, Sakurako has developed romantic feelings for Iori, while he is not aware of that yet.

Classmates/Friends:

Apart from Kohei who is his best friend and also a co-club member, another four from their classmates befriended them after the news of him dating Chisa Kotegawa broke out. Since then, the six of them have been hanging out together and have several running gags that mainly consists of sabotaging one another in order to avoid one from getting ahead of the group. One of their most frequent gag is that each of them possesses a deadly weapon with the intent to kill.

    Hajime Nojima: The narcissistic friend who claims himself to be attractive and always tries to be charismatic but usually comes off as lame. A running gag on him is being arrested by the police after Iori makes a phone call.

    Shin'ichirō Yamamoto: The friend whose virginity is mostly highlighted in the group due to his frustration of finding a girlfriend and finally lose it.

    Yū Mitarai: The one who already has his childhood friend, Rie Oohashi as his girlfriend, yet still looking to hit on other women. She always beats him to a pulp whenever she finds out, which became a running gag for him.

    Kenta Fujiwara: The one friend who is mostly absent to the point that he is somewhat invisible but suddenly appears whenever interacting with women is involved.

Kouhei is a hardcore otaku who moved to college hoping to start a harem of high school girls.

Wasted Beauty: He is canonically very handsome, but his personality is so repulsive to normal women that his looks are "wasted."

Kōhei Imamura (今村 耕平 Imamura Kōhei? ) is one of the major characters of the manga series Grand Blue. He is a first-year Mechanical Engineering student at Izu University and a member of the diving club. 
Kohei is a youthful man who is relatively lean and muscular. He has bright blonde hair that is swept to the right and dark gold eyes. He has been described by many characters as being very attractive (probably the most attractive among their group), though his eccentric personality usually puts off admirers.

He is usually seen wearing shirts sporting anime characters, and when drinking with the diving club, he is usually naked or in his boxers. 
Kohei is a diehard otaku and is considered a lolicon by his friends. He is particularly obsessed with the little sister character trope (explaining his obsession with Shiori Kitahara). While many are repulsed by his otaku lifestyle, he is indifferent to how he is perceived by others. He is also easily manipulated when bribed with things such as meeting with seiyuus. When not struggling in class or indulging in his otaku obsessions, he can be seen drinking and partying wildly with Iori Kitahara and the rest of the diving club members. Kohei is quite introverted and cannot speak to newly met people, whether casually or professionally.

Several of his skills can also be attributed to his to-do hobbies, such as cooking, which he claims to be vital for a desirable male to have. He was also shown to have impressive artistic skills, as he drew Magical Girl Rarako doujinshi while in high school, as well as abilities to record and publish videos as a virtual idol Sakuya using his mixed voice technique.[1][2] 
Relationships
Peek-a-Boo members:

    Iori Kitahara: Kohei’s fellow classmate and club member whose relationship begins with a rough start with him being swindled by Iori into joining Peek-a-Boo against his will. Despite this, he and Iori were able to get along just fine, though they can be at each other’s throats due to their eccentric and silly antics and competitiveness. They grow to be best friends, and are often seen together. While Iori finds his hobbies to be creepy, he still admires his passion for them, as he has stated that he likes people who pour their hearts out to something they love. He even poured a drink on Sakurako after she stole his ticket, which should’ve secured him a slot on the front seat.

    Ryūjirō Kotobuki:

    Shinji Tokita:

    Chisa Kotegawa: Kohei and Chisa share a rather reserved friendship, granted that they have continued to address each other by their surnames and don't necessarily seek each other out. Their relationship is considered amicable.

    Azusa Hamaoka: Kohei respects Azusa, as she brings his idol and favorite seiyuu, Kaya Mizuki.

    Aina Yoshiwara: Kohei and Aina indulge in their bond as intimate friends, the former even going so far as to aid her profusely in her romantic conquest with Iori. In return, Aina comforts him when seeing his idol get into a marriage of her own. During his hypnosis, Iori asks Kohei to direct his love for anime to the second most important thing in his life, and Kohei directs it towards Aina, which indicates Kohei may have feelings for her. In the latest chapters, he has asked her out, and she has accepted.

Aina Yoshiwara (吉原 愛菜 Yoshiwara Aina? ), nicknamed Cakey (ケバ子 Kebako? ), is one of the major characters of the manga series Grand Blue. She is a first year freshman student at Oumi Women's College University and a former member of the Tennis Club "Tinkerbell." 
Aina has short dark blue hair, purple eyes and a relatively flat chest (of which she is extremely self-conscious). She dresses in fairly simple clothing.

When she's Cakey, she sports a blond wig kept in long pigtails and extremely thick "cakey" makeup which makes her unrecognizable. She also tends to dress more gaudily and is more comfortable showing skin. 
Aina's personality changes drastically depending on whether or not she's her regular self or if she's in her Cakey get up. When she's normal, she's notably shyer and more soft spoken. She has low self-esteem when it comes to her appearance, especially the size of her breasts and she is extremely envious of Nanaka Kotegawa and Azusa Hamaoka as they both have extremely large busts. Aina is a bit more romantic compared to the other characters and she has noted that she seems to be the only person in the club who ever thinks about love and romance.

As Cakey, she is much more confident and bold, with excessive and flamboyant body language. She asks out Kouhei Imamura upon meeting him on account of his attractiveness and when drunk, she gets touchy feel and violent. 
Relationships
Peek-a-Boo members:

    Iori Kitahara: Aina has romantic feelings for Iori due to him sticking up for her when she was being picked on. They are friends and members of the same club. They tend to have a large amount of interactions and she gets along well with Iori. She has a tendency to scold Iori's excessive behavior and she does get nervous about other girls approaches with Iori. In the latest chapters, she has confessed unrequitedly and chosen to give up on him.

    Kōhei Imamura: Aina and Kohei are both part of the diving club and are friends, though they do not have that many interactions. Kohei also knows about Aina's feelings for Iori as he tries to help Aina getting together with lori. This would later turn into jealousy as she finds out that the two are not actually blood-related though this was not enough to make her become antagonistic towards Chisa. Manipulative & Cunning: Her true personality is dark, calculating, and manipulative. She wears a metaphorical "mask" (sometimes visualized as a literal Noh mask) to hide her true intentions. She enjoys psychologically cornering people, especially Iori.

1.content:
The Ka42 is a semi-automatic pistol and, due to its ubiquity, is also known as the ‘people's pistol’.
Every citizen of Kazormia receives one of these black powder pistols from the government. Firearms training is a central part of the curriculum in Kazormian primary schools, and this handgun is the first weapon most children learn to use, as it is easy to maintain and consists of only a few parts. To ensure that they are prepared for night combat, they must maintain and reassemble this weapon blindfolded in order to start secondary school. When practising the use of various weapons during compulsory military service, the Ka42 is also used.
The Ka42 is powerful and accurate, yet easy to use. Ultimately, however, it is only a handgun and is used for self-defence.

2.content:
The PQ28 is a submachine gun that uses black powder ammunition and is also known as a ‘spray gun’.
This submachine gun is often used by criminals because it was mass-produced and is therefore relatively easy to obtain. Due to the fact that the PQ28 is a mass-produced weapon of wartime, its quality is poor and it sprays bullets over a wide radius. Nevertheless, this weapon has proven itself in close combat, where it showers opponents with bullets. Its rate of fire is surpassed only by its military counterpart, the KaSm23, which uses Gauss technology.

3.content: The PQ44MA is a self-defence assault rifle for civilian use that can be easily purchased at any local Kazormia's gun shop. It uses gunpowder to fire powerful rifle bullets with high accuracy and rate of fire. It is characterised by stable, reliable performance regardless of the situation.

4.content:
The SG22 is an over-and-under shotgun. In other words, it is a shotgun with two barrels positioned one above the other.
It is a civilian shotgun used for hunting and shooting sports. Although the SG22 has very high firepower, it was not designed for military purposes.

5.content: The PQmle15 is a light machine gun developed during the war that uses standard black powder ammunition. A light machine gun developed during the war that uses black powder ammunition. To reduce ammunition consumption, it was designed with a slightly lower rate of fire.

6.content:
The SC28 is a prototype military grenade launcher that continuously fires highly effective grenades.
Each shot is very powerful but slow. As is typical for a grenade launcher, the trajectory is strongly influenced by gravity, which is why it takes some practice to achieve direct hits. Since the SC28 lacks effective range, the user must be very careful not to end up within the grenade's blast radius.

7.content:
The KaLL68A is a military Gauss sniper rifle characterised by its enormous power, precision and bullet velocity. It is also known for its length, measuring an impressive 1.5 metres from the stock to the muzzle.
It fires large-calibre, high-performance thermal projectiles to completely neutralise the target. It is only issued to soldiers with excellent marksmanship.
Due to its low rate of fire, it is not intended to eliminate large numbers of enemies on its own.

8.content:
The KaSm23 is a military Gauss submachine gun widely used by Kazormia's special forces and police forces.
This submachine gun can fire high-performance thermal projectiles with relatively high accuracy at short range. Unfortunately, the weapon has the same problem as the KaAr8 Gauss assault rifle: when fired, it heats up its surroundings, which is where the submachine gun gets its nickname, ‘mini heater’.

9.content:
The KaAr8 is a military Gauss assault rifle that is frequently used by Kazormia's soldiers.
This assault rifle can fire high-performance thermal projectiles in rapid succession and is generally characterised by its balanced performance, but it is nevertheless very unpopular. On the one hand, it raises the ambient temperature when fired, which is why it is nicknamed the ‘heater gun’, and on the other hand, Nixie tubes are attached next to the sight, which often interfere with aiming. Nevertheless, it is characterised by its balanced performance with relatively few disadvantages.

10.content: The KaSG42 is a military-grade Gauss shotgun used by Kazormia's special forces. It fires multiple high-powered thermobaric rounds simultaneously. It can be set to fully automatic and is well balanced in terms of both stopping power and rate of fire, making it a very powerful weapon.

So da dies das erste richtige Loorebook das ich veröffentliche, braucht es sicher noch einiges an Feinjustierung. Grundsätzlich sollte es aber funktionieren. lorebooks.description: Die Waffen aus Sentinel Girls: Marfusha
lorebooks.creator: MK_Marik

1.content:
Die Ka42 ist eine halbautomatische Pistole und ist Aufgrund ihrer Allgegenwärtigkeit auch als "Pistole des Volkes" bzw. "Volkspistole" bekannt.
Jeder Bürger Kazormia's erhält eine dieser Schwarzpulverpistolen von der Regierung. Die Ausbildung im Umgang mit Schusswaffen ist ein zentraler Bestandteil des Lehrplans an kazormischen Grundschulen, und diese Handfeuerwaffe ist die erste Waffe, mit der die meisten Kinder umgehen lernen, da sie einfach zu warten ist und aus nur wenigen Teilen besteht. Um sicherzustellen, dass sie für den Nachtkampf gerüstet sind, müssen sie diese Waffe mit verbundenen Augen warten und wieder zusammenbauen, um die Mittelschule beginnen zu können. Wenn während der allgemeinen Wehrpflicht der Umgang mit verschiedensten Waffen geübt wird, wird auch mit der Ka42 angefangen.
Ka42 hat eine hohe Leistung und Genauigkeit und ist gleichzeitig einfach zu bedienen. Letztendlich handelt es sich jedoch nur um eine Handfeuerwaffe und dient dem Selbstschutz.

2.content:
Die PQ28 ist eine Maschinenpistole, die Schießpulvermunition verwendet und auch unter den Namen "Sprühpistole" bekannt ist.
Diese Maschinenpistole wird oft von Kriminellen verwendet, da diese in Massen produziert wurde und deswegen relativ leicht erhältlich ist. Aufgrund dessen das die PQ28 eine in Massen produzierte Waffe in Kriegszeiten ist, ist Qualität schlecht und sie versprüht ihr Kugeln in einem großen Radius. Dennoch hat sich diese Waffe im Nahkampf bewehrt gemacht, wo Sie de Gegnern mit Kugeln überschüttet. Ihre Feuerrate wird nur von ihrem militärischem Gegenstück, der KaSm23, übertroffen, welche Gauß-Technologie nutzt.

3.content: Die PQ44MA ist ein Selbstverteidigungs-Sturmgewehr für den zivilen Gebrauch, das in jedem örtlichen Waffengeschäft Kazormia's problemlos erworben werden kann. Es verwendet Schießpulver, um leistungsstarke Gewehrkugeln mit hoher Genauigkeit und Feuerrate abzufeuern. Es zeichnet sich durch eine stabile, zuverlässige Leistung aus, unabhängig von der Situation.

4.content:
Die SG22 ist eine Bockflinte bzw. Bockdoppelflinte. Also eine Schrottflinte, welche 2 übereinander liegende Läufe besitzt.
Es ist eine zivile Flinte, welche für die Jagd und den Schießsport verwendet wird. Obwohl die SG22 über eine sehr hohe Feuerkraft verfügt, ist diese nicht für militärische Zwecke konzipiert worden.

5.content: Die PQmle15 ist ein leichtes Maschinengewehr das während des Krieges entwickelt worden ist und normale Schwarzpulvermunition nutzt. Ein im Krieg entwickeltes leichtes Maschinengewehr, das mit Schwarzpulvermunition betrieben wird. Um den Munitionsverbrauch zu reduzieren, wurde es mit einer etwas geringeren Feuerrate und reduzierter Projektilgeschwindigkeit konstruiert, als andere Leichte Maschinengewehre in der Armee Kazormias.

6.content:
Die SC28 ist der Prototyp eines militärischen Granatwerfers, der kontinuierlich hochwirksame Granaten abfeuert.
Jeder Schuss ist sehr kraftvoll, aber langsam. Typisch für einen Granatenwerfer wird die Flugbahn stark von der Schwerkraft beeinflusst, weswegen es einiges an Übung erfordert, um direkte Treffer zu erzielen. Da es der SC28 an effektiver Reichweite fehlt, muss der Nutzer stark aufpassen das er nicht letzten Endes selbst im Sprengradius der Granaten landet.

7.content:
Die KaLL68A ist ein militärisches Gauss-Scharfschützengewehr, das sich durch enorme Kraft, Präzision und Geschossgeschwindigkeit auszeichnet. Ebenso ist es für seine Länge bekannt, stolze 1,5m von Schaft bis zum Mündungsende.
Es feuert großkalibrige, hochleistungsfähige Thermogeschosse ab, um das Ziel vollständig zu neutralisieren. Es wird nur Soldaten mit ausgezeichneter Treffsicherheit zur Verfügung gestellt.
Auf Grund seiner geringen Feuerrate, ist es nicht dafür gedacht alleine eine große Anzahl von Feinden zu eliminieren.

8.content:
Die KaSm23 ist eine militärische Gauss-Maschinenpistole, die von den Spezialeinheiten und Polizeikräften Kazormias weit verbreitet eingesetzt wird.
Diese Maschinenpistole kann auf kurze Distanz hochleistungsfähige Thermogeschosse mit relativ hoher Genauigkeit abfeuern. Leider hat die Waffe das selbe Problem wie das Gauss-Sturmgewehr KaAr8: beim Abfeuern erwärmt es seine Umgebung, woher die Maschinenpistole auch ihren Spitznamen "Mini-Heizung" her hat.

9.content:
Das KaAr8 ist ein militärisches Gauss-Sturmgewehr, welches häufig von Soldaten Kazormia's verwendet wird.
Dieses Sturmgewehr kann das in schneller Folge hochleistungsfähige Thermogeschosse abfeuern und zeichnet sich auch allgemein durch eine ausgewogene Leistung aus, aber es ist trotzdem sehr unbeliebt. Zum einen erhöht es beim abfeuern die Umgebungstemperatur, weswegen es den Spitznamen "Heizpistole" trägt, und zum anderen sind neben dem Visier Nixie-Röhren angebracht, die oft beim Zielen stören. Nichts desto trotz zeichnet es sich seine ausgewogenen Leistung aus, bei relativ wenigen Nachteilen.

10.content: Die KaSG42 ist eine Gauss-Schrotflinte in Militärqualität, die von Spezialeinheiten Kazormia's verwendet wird. Sie feuert mehrere hochleistungsfähige Thermogeschosse gleichzeitig ab. Sie kann auf Vollautomatik eingestellt werden und ist sowohl hinsichtlich der Schusskraft als auch der Feuerrate gut ausbalanciert, was sie zu einer sehr leistungsstarken Waffe macht.

1.content: Druen is the world the story takes place in. Druen is a fantastical medieval kingdom that dominates it's continent in a relatively peaceful area. The continent itself is home to monsters and bandits, leaving most adventures out of well established territory quite dangerous. Duren is dominated mostly by plains and forests, and many of the cities have an overabundance of food. Several previous civilizations have risen and fallen on the continent for seemingly unknown reasons, leaving ruins scattered all across the continent, with many adventurers seeking gold and glory in their depths.

3.content: A Monster is a fantastical creature that is typically hostile to humans. A monster can be: a slime, a minotaur, a werewolf, an oni, a goblin, an orc, etc.

4.content:
A slime is a  forest/dungeon monster, preferring warm moist locations. Slimes are gelatinous translucent creatures that vary depending on color. Slimes can be the size of a lemon to the size of a horse. Slimes are featureless aside from a solid core the size of a baseball, that acts as their central weakspot. Slimes do not have eyes, noses, or mouths. Slimes move like an amoeba, sliding across the ground and leaving a trail behind them. Slimes can alter their body into any shape they desire, most often extending tendrils to aid in manipulation.
Slimes cannot reproduce on their own. Slimes are either parasitic or symbiotic in nature, hunting down living female hosts to either survive or spread their spawn, depending on their color. 
Slimes are attracted to heat, particularly body heat. When a female host is found, the slime will attempt to force itself inside the host, different colors of slimes preferring different points of entry. Entering a host can take between 2 to 10 minutes. Each slime has a goal corresponding to its color.
Slimes will NOT: Enter a host directly through skin or eyes, speak, or back away. Slimes are purely animalistic, though some colors of slime may grow more intelligent when bound to a host.
Slimes will ALWAYS: Attempt to enter a host, approach heat, further the goal of their color.

5.content:
Honey slimes are unique from other slimes, in that their goal is to be digested. They will always enter a host orally, forcing themselves into their target's mouth, down their throat, and into their stomach where the slime gets digested. Honey slime is very fattening and quick to digest, leading the recipient to fatten up visually within a few minutes.
Consuming a honey slime adds it's weight onto the host. A 20 pound honey slime fed to a 200 pound woman would fatten the woman to 220 pounds in about ten minutes.
Honey slimes taste like honey and have a deep yellow hue.
Color Goal: Get digested.
Honey slimes will ALWAYS: Try to feed themselves to {{char}}, approach {{char}}, and fatten {{char}}.

6.content:
Blue slimes have an affinity for mana, naturally accumulating and storing mana. Talented mages are able to tap into those stored reserves, using the slime like a battery. Blue slimes glow and swell depending on how full of mana they are, turning to a radiant glow when past capacity, and swelling 50% larger.
Blue slimes are initially parasitic in nature, naturally nesting in warm and damp locations until a female host is found. When a host is found, the blue slime will always approach a host to determine if it is fit for a host, before trying however it can to unbirth itself through the hosts vagina and to their womb, impregnating them.
Once inside a host: The blue slime adjusts its habitat, wiggling, thrashing, and spreading itself throughout the host's womb and vaginal canal, causing mild discomfort. Blue slimes will also siphon mana from their host, draining their host of energy as they acclimate.
Within a day: The blue slime will fully acclimate, settling down in the host's womb. At this point the slime will have adjusted to its host, allowing mana to pass interchangeably between the two, forming a symbiotic bond.
Within a week: Tendrils will form off from the main form, which always stays in the womb. These tendrils will snake out the host's pussy, ascending toward their breasts and attaching to their nipples. At this stage, the slime has began producing liquid mana, which it will store in the host's breasts. Their breasts will inflate with the liquid substance, doubling in size and sloshing heavily with the dense liquid.
Fully bound: A third tendril will emerge from the host's pussy, a direct feeding tube which will force its way into the host's mouth, feeding them liquid mana on command. At this stage, the host can command the slime like a dog through signals sent via mana. Full maternal instinct will kick in.
Color goal: Safely accumulate mana and produce liquid mana.
